user_reviews.Name: Julie Franke
user_reviews.Description:
Well, Maia Beach Bar in Da Nang certainly wins on looks — modern beachy chic and that ridiculously beautiful beachfront view that makes you consider forgetting there are other places in Da Nanag waiting to be explored.

But look, I’d love to say the service matched the scenery... sadly, we practically had to wave like we were flagging down a rescue boat just to get refills and we were seated right near the bar. I ordered the Octavo Arte Sauvignon Blanc (Spain) for 155,000 dong — lovely drop, crisp and clean — but the wine list is pretty slim - Riesling was the only other white, and that’s a hard no from me. But I totally get this as wine does not seem very popular here in Da Nang as the cocktails are the same price if not cheaper - so was very thankful that even though the wine list was minimal - the Sauvignon Blanc was very very nice.

The snacks? We ordered the olives and peanuts. The olives were your garden-variety green ones straight from a jar but the peanuts were nice. I actually love olives and order them whenever they're on the menu - so I know there are places in Da Nang that make sure they serve up fabulous olives - one place had 3 different olive types and they were marinated perfectly - that was what I was expecting here. We didn't try anything else from their food menu - so maybe the food is great - we went elsewhere for dinner.

Now, a little drama to add to the mix — there was a very very drunk man dancing and carrying on right where the female DJ was performing, and at one point he nearly toppled into her. He was trying to talk to her, even gave her a plate of food at one point - she was not interested and I don't know how she kept so calm and polite. The staff watched but didn’t step in until he got loud and argumentative later. I get that they were trying to avoid a scene, but honestly, he should’ve been quietly ushered away before it ever got to that point. It was uncomfortable to watch, especially as a woman.

I am guessing without the drama of a very drunk patron - the service from staff might have been more attentive - I guess a return visit is in order to find out.

On a brighter note, the music was fantastic — the DJs knew their stuff - and the music was loud but not loud enough to stop conversation. Really enjoyed their sets.

Great tunes, great view, great wine but they really need to lift their game on service and how to deal with very drunk customers. A bit more care, and I'm sure Maia Beach Bar could be as fabulous as it looks.

user_reviews.Name: Suraj Goswami
user_reviews.Description:
I recently had the pleasure of dining at Himalaya Indian Restaurant in Da Nang, and as a pure vegetarian, it was honestly one of the most satisfying meals I’ve had in Vietnam.
I tried their Gujarati Thali, and it was absolutely exceptional. The thali was beautifully presented and offered a perfect balance of flavors—sweet, spicy, tangy, and comforting, just like authentic Gujarati cuisine should be. Every item tasted fresh and homemade, from the rich dal and flavorful vegetable curries to the soft rotis and perfectly cooked rice. The subtle sweetness in the dishes truly captured the essence of traditional Gujarati food.
What impressed me most was the authenticity. It didn’t feel like a “restaurant version” of Indian food—it felt like a home-cooked meal. The portion size was generous, making it great value for money, and ideal if you want to experience a variety of dishes in one meal.
The staff were warm, attentive, and genuinely welcoming, which made the experience even better. The ambiance is clean, peaceful, and perfect for a relaxed meal.
If you’re looking for pure vegetarian Indian food in Da Nang, especially something authentic like a Gujarati Thali, this place is a must-visit. Highly recommended!

user_reviews.Name: Her Dolce Vita
user_reviews.Description:
*edit* Updated review – very disappointing

Sadly, my second visit was the complete opposite, uncomfortable, disrespectful, and upsetting.

I arrived ahead of my partner and TRIED to order 2 portions of the Sichuan chicken for both of us, just like we did before. But instead of simply taking my order, the staff member questioned me over and over: “Are you sure?”, “It’s a lot of food”, “How many friends are coming?”, and kept pressing despite me explaining that my partner was coming shortly.

She left without placing the order. When my partner arrived and confirmed the order again, she repeated that it was too much food. I was so frustrated I finally said, “Yes, I’m fat. What now?” only then did she stop and take the order.

It was completely unnecessary. I came in wanting to support this restaurant, and I even planned to recommend it on my food review YouTube channel. But after this experience, I honestly can’t recommend it or say I’d return.

*old review*

*AC & card payment available* Just WOW amazing, authentic Chinese food! We tried so many dishes and loved them all. Everything was full of flavour and cooked perfectly. Most dishes are quite spicy, so if you’re sensitive to heat, it’s worth asking for less spice when you order. Definitely a must-visit if you’re craving real Chinese cuisine! Definitely coming back soon! 🥰

user_reviews.Name: Precious Quy Le
user_reviews.Description:
Stray into the very stylish interior designed Trattoria and be treated to the very best Italian cuisine that Vietnam has on offer. Don Cipriani’s at the Furama Resort Danang, the ideal setting for “al fresco” pool-side dining or in air conditioned comfort, will serve traditional Italian cuisine with flare and with an intriguing slant take using the very best of local and imported ingredients.

The featured traditional Italian cuisine will be artistically presented by our experienced chefs in a relaxing and friendly ambience with a cool breeze wafting in off the pacific ocean.

title: Maharaja Indian Restaurant Đà Nẵng ( since 2016)
address: 09 An Thượng 2, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam
plus_code: 26XW+RV Ngu Hanh Son, Da Nang, Vietnam
description: Traditional Indian dishes, curries & veggies are served in an informal space with outdoor seating.

owner.name: Maharaja Indian Restaurant Đà Nẵng ( since 2016) (Owner)

user_reviews.Name: The Candid Critic
user_reviews.Description:
Maharaja Restaurant in Da Nang turned out to be one of our favorite Indian food spots during the trip, and we ended up visiting twice. On our first visit, we ordered butter tandoori roti and kadhai paneer, medium spicy. The paneer was cooked really well, flavorful without being overpowering, and the spice level was just right. What stood out the most was the roti, it was served warm, fresh, and soft, the kind you can easily keep ordering without getting tired of it.

Because the first meal was so satisfying, we went back again. This time, we tried a simple dal tadka and vegetable jalfrezi, again medium spicy, along with butter tandoori roti. The dal tadka was comforting and well balanced, and the jalfrezi had a good mix of vegetables with nice flavors. Once again, the quality stayed consistent, which is always a good sign.

The food is genuinely good and very affordable compared to many other Indian restaurants in Da Nang. If you’re finding other places expensive or hit or miss, Maharaja is a dependable choice. It offers good quality food, fair pricing, and decent service. Definitely a go to restaurant if you’re craving Indian food while in Da Nang.

title: India0Miles Indian Restaurant Danang
address: Inside Barett Urban Zone, E11 Trần Bạch Đằng, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 50000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Tharun KATKURI
user_reviews.Description:
Tried something off-menu today and it turned out to be a total win! 🙌

Asked the chef to customize a cheese garlic naan with some veggies, and they absolutely nailed it. The naan was perfectly soft with a slight crisp on the edges, loaded with melted cheese and that rich garlic flavor. The added veggies gave it a fresh, slightly crunchy balance that made every bite even better.

And the real game changer? A sprinkle of chaat masala on top—it seriously elevated the taste with that tangy, slightly spicy kick. 🤤

Really appreciate when a kitchen is flexible and willing to go beyond the menu to create something special. This one’s definitely worth asking for if you visit! 🔥

title: Bánh Xèo Bà Dưỡng
address: 280/23 Hoàng Diệu, Hải Châu, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Joe C
user_reviews.Description: Very good banh xeo in the Da Nang area. We had banh xeo, bo ne, and nem lui. All three were delicious. The prices were reasonable, not too expensive. The location is a little odd. The sign to the restaurant is on the main street but you have to walk down to the end of the alley to get there. It's worth the try. Recommended.
user_reviews.text_original: Very good banh xeo in the Da Nang area. We had banh xeo, bo ne, and nem lui. All three were delicious. The prices were reasonable, not too expensive. The location is a little odd. The sign to the restaurant is on the main street but you have to walk down to the end of the alley to get there. It's worth the try. Recommended.

user_reviews.Name: James Korin
user_reviews.Description:
This place is amazing! Tucked way back in a long alley is this Banh Xeo gem. This place was teeming with activity and patrons devouring the delicious and flavorful Banh Xeo.  The food is immediately brought to your table and is ready to be rolled into the best bite of Banh Xeo you have had in your life.  It's a very simple menu because all they specialize in is Banh Xeo! And they do a damn good job at it. The dipping sauce that goes with it is just amazing. The complexity of flavors compliments the Banh Xeo perfectly.

This place is often duplicated but don't fall for it. Look for the sign. You won't be disappointed!

user_reviews.Name: Suzette Mcartney
user_reviews.Description:
I’m not sure what else I can add that hasn’t already been said in all the reviews, but here are my thoughts. We’ve eaten plenty of bánh xèo during our month-plus stay in Da Nang and were curious to see if this place really lived up to the hype, especially since you can find bánh xèo much cheaper away from the tourist areas.

I have to admit — it was genuinely delicious. We visited twice, and both times the restaurant was mostly filled with locals rather than just tourists or “influencers,” which is always a good sign.

The bánh xèo was full of flavour, and we also ordered the grilled beef and skewers. The grilled beef was juicy, tender, and packed with flavour — we actually preferred it over the skewers. The warm dipping sauce was a highlight; it’s more like a rich gravy than a traditional peanut sauce and contains ingredients like liver, which gives it a unique depth and richness.

Definitely worth a visit if you’re in Da Nang.

user_reviews.Name: Jimmy Su
user_reviews.Description:
This was my first time here and was recommended by friends and family. The sauce was what makes everything work with each other.

Definitely get the banh xeo and the meat skewers and skip the grilled beef.

Service is your typical busy restaurant, where you need to flag them down. Ambiance is really loud and busy during lunch hours.

But overall I will come back when I’m in Da Nang.

user_reviews.Name: Tashin Jiracharoenvongsa
user_reviews.Description:
This restaurant offers a focused menu with only 4 main dishes, and I highly recommend ordering all of them because every single one is absolutely delicious. This has been my favorite Vietnamese dining experience throughout my entire Da Nang trip. The flavors are authentic and truly hit the spot!

Menu Highlights:
- Bánh Xèo (Vietnamese Pancake) — 4.5/5: Generally tasty.
- Nem Lụi (Grilled Meat Roll) — 4.5/5: Generally tasty.
- Bò Nướng Lá Lốt (Grilled Beef in Piper Lolot Leaves) — 5/5: This was the standout! It has a deep, mellow flavor with a subtle hint of fermented fish sauce that rounds everything out perfectly.
- Bún Thịt Nướng (Noodles with Grilled Meat): A simple grilled pork served over noodles.

Overall the food here is highly recommended. What sets this place apart from others is their signature dipping sauce and the Bò Nướng Lá Lốt.

Furthermore, the restaurant is clean and serves generous portions of fresh vegetables, making it a great spot for vegetable lovers! It's a must-visit restaurant in Da Nang!

user_reviews.Name: Anita L M
user_reviews.Description:
It took us long enough to come here and try the Banh Xeo. We ordered three items out of the four that were on the menu.

Banh Xeo is what they're known for, and it's really good. Crunchy and filled with pork, shrimp and bean sprouts.

Nem Lui, meat on a stick is very tasty and I highly suggest you order some of these. They come in orders of 5, 10 or more  so if you're by yourself or in a small party, you can still enjoy them.

Grilled meat with la lot leaves was my favorite. So flavorful yet tender. I don't think you should miss out on this.

Absolutely tasty and I can't wait to come back again. TBH, I think I might even skip the Banh Xeo next time and just simply focus on the meats!

user_reviews.Name: pavan br
user_reviews.Description:
My recent encounter with an authentic Vietnamese dish transcended mere dining it became a memorable lesson in warmth and culinary delight. It began with a moment of delightful bewilderment. Faced with an unfamiliar yet tantalizing plate, I found myself unsure of the proper way to approach it. How does one truly savor this? My hesitation, however, was short-lived.
The answer came not from a menu or a quick search, but from the very people around me. Their friendliness, a quality I often hear about but rarely experience so intimately, became immediately apparent. Seeing my slight confusion, they didn't just offer advice; they actively engaged, patiently showing me the ropes. In fact, they went a step further, demonstrating the preparation and even assembling the dish for me, turning a potentially awkward moment into a shared experience.
And the taste? Truly amazing. The symphony of flavors, each ingredient playing its part, was exquisite. But what truly elevated the dish to magnificent heights was the sauce. It was a revelation, a perfect counterpoint and complement that brought everything together in a harmonious, unforgettable way. This wasn't just a meal; it was an immersion into a culture where food is shared, and hospitality is a language spoken with genuine kindness.

title: Danang Tower
address: 341 Đ. Trần Hưng Đạo, Phường, An Hải, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: HYERAN HONG
user_reviews.Description: The steak cut on a hot stone plate is juicy and soft in texture. It becomes tough when it cools down, but it is so delicious that you can eat it up quickly without having time to cool down. In the evening, the view of the Han River and the city through the glass windows is so beautiful and the atmosphere is great. The staff is very friendly and responds quickly. The stir-fried morning glory is highly recommended. It is a large portion and very delicious. I also like the sweet and sour taste of the pinkish radish pickles.

user_reviews.Name: Doron William
user_reviews.Description:
I stopped by for a drink — great experience overall. Friendly and helpful staff, stunning view, and fair prices for the quality. Just a heads-up: as far as I understood from the receipt and brief chat with staff, no service charge (e.g. 5%) is included. That’s unusual for higher-end places in Vietnam, where it’s often added automatically.

There’s a tip box placed subtly on the right side, which I almost missed. So if you want to leave a tip, check your bill — and bring cash. If my understanding is incorrect, I’m happy to update this review after clarification from Melia.

Golden sunset, city lights, and a table full of happiness. 💛
🔥 Grilled prawns loaded with crispy garlic & shallots – flavor explosion in every bite!
🍚 Tender beef fried rice with crunchy garlic chips – comfort food done right.
🌇 And that view… Dragon Bridge and the whole city glowing under the evening sky.

💜 Perfect food, perfect vibe, perfect moment.
If you’re in Da Nang, this spot is a must! 🌴💫

title: KHONG Vegan Kitchen
address: 81 Phan Tôn, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam
plus_code: 362V+QH Ngu Hanh Son, Da Nang, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Giuseppe Bisaccia
user_reviews.Description:
I’ve tried several vegan restaurants in Vietnam, but I can honestly say this one is the absolute best. The dishes are incredibly tasty and generous in portion size. Ever since I discovered it, I’ve been coming here for both lunch and dinner throughout my entire stay in Da Nang.

I would’ve come for breakfast too, but they open at 11, which is a shame, because their fruit bowls are absolutely amazing.

Among my favorite dishes, the Nutty Peanut Noodles are definitely in first place, super creamy and full of flavor. I also highly recommend the Peanut Chickpea Curry, Peanut Miso Soup and the Summer Rolls.

Plus, the staff is incredibly kind and the owner is absolutely lovely. Highly recommended!

user_reviews.reply_text_original:
Dear Giuseppe Bisaccia, thank you so much for this incredibly thoughtful review. 🌿
We’re truly honored that Khong Vegan Restaurant became your favorite during your stay in Da Nang.
It means a lot to hear how much you enjoyed the Nutty Peanut Noodles, Peanut Chickpea Curry, Peanut Miso Soup, and our Summer Rolls — and we’re especially happy our fruit bowls left such a great impression too.
Your kind words about our team and owner genuinely warm our hearts. 
We hope to welcome you back whenever you return to Da Nang! 🌱

user_reviews.Name: Rebekah Uhrick
user_reviews.Description: Absolutely delicious food! We've eaten here four times in the past two weeks, trying the crispy mushroom appetizer, peanut noodles, the "beef" stew (every dish is vegan, not to worry), mushroom burger, and the lemongrass and the teriyaki tofu with rice bowls. All is fresh, perfectly cooked, nicely presented, and hearty. The service is quick and friendly, and the prices are very reasonable! Highly recommend.

user_reviews.Name: Antanetta Marshall
user_reviews.Description:
I have been having food from here delivered so frequently that the person on the other end sent a message of appreciation through the grab app my last order. 😅
I have yet to visit the establishment in person but It's safe to say I like it quite a bit and will.
The things I've tried so far:
Crispy Mushroom Taco
*Tasty-It's good as it comes BUT add their cheese sauce to it...thank me later
Cheesy fries
*Tasty - My favorite kind of fries
Lentil Curry
*Tasty
Nutty Peanut Noodles
*Not bad just not my preference
Cinnamon Latte
*YES, get it.
Vegan Brownie
*Tasty
Banana Bread
*Not bad but not my preference. It's light on the sweetness and could use a light glaze or maple syrup in the mix imo
Crispy Mushroom Burger
*Tasty
Summer Noodles
*Not bad but also not my preference
Everything tasty I've ordered multiple times or anticipate ordering again. Hope this helps😆

user_reviews.Name: Tarah Batts
user_reviews.Description: Khong Vegan Kitchen is such a lovely find in Da Nang. Everything we tried was full of flavour and beautifully balanced. The hot chocolate on oat milk was rich and smooth without being overly sweet. The lentil curry was warm and well-spiced, and Khong’s stew noodle soup was absolutely delicious. The juice tasted freshly squeezed and the soft background music created a calm, relaxing atmosphere that made the whole experience even more enjoyable. A great spot for nourishing vegan food with a calming ambiance - highly recommend.

user_reviews.reply_text_original:
Dear Tarah Batts, thank you for leaving a review.
We truly appreciate you finding Khong a delightful discovery in Da Nang and taking the time to experience our food and ambiance so thoughtfully. 
We're thrilled you enjoyed everything from our hot chocolate with oat milk and lentil curry to our stew noodle soup, and that you appreciated our fresh juice and cozy atmosphere.
Your thoughtful recommendation and kind words mean the world to us. 
We'll strive to ensure your next visit brings another comfortable and satisfying dining experience. 🌱

user_reviews.Name: Anika
user_reviews.Description:
I was really hungry and found this vegan restaurant by chance. I ordered the vegan beef stew, and it was absolutely amazing. It’s real comfort food with hearty chunks of potato and carrot in a creamy pumpkin-based soup, with some soy pieces for texture. The side dish was brown rice, and it matched perfectly.

The menu looks nice and the staff were friendly and helpful. The only minus point is that they charge a 3 percent fee for card payment, which I think is unnecessary.

Overall, delicious food and a pleasant place, just be aware of the card charge

title: ISH - The Indian Restaurant
address: 507 Đ. Trần Hưng Đạo, An Hải, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Vicky Somaiya
user_reviews.Description:
We came to ISH Indian Restaurant for dinner on the first day of our Vietnam trip in Da Nang.

We ordered beer and veg biryani. The biryani tasted really good and was satisfying after a long day of travel. The staff was polite, welcoming, and the ambience was pleasant.

Overall, it was a nice dining experience and a great start to our Vietnam trip.
user_reviews.text_original:

Overall, it was a nice dining experience and a great start to our Vietnam trip.

user_reviews.Name: NiniJam
user_reviews.Description: A lovely restaurant serving authentic Indian cuisine. Their chicken biryani was superb and the waiter even gave us the option of spiciness level, it didn’t disappoint! The portion is massive and comes with tangy vege raita, a biryani bowl can feed 2 people so order conservatively first before adding more if you need to. Location is premium, right along the main street of the Dragon Bridge (fire-breathing dragon every Friday and weekends, 9PM). Wish I stayed longer in Da Nang to have time for another round of ISH!
user_reviews.text_original: A lovely restaurant serving authentic Indian cuisine. Their chicken biryani was superb and the waiter even gave us the option of spiciness level, it didn’t disappoint! Location is premium, right along the main street of the Dragon Bridge (fire-breathing dragon every Friday and weekends, 9PM). Wish I stayed longer in Da Nang to have time for another round of ISH!

user_reviews.Name: Jack Nugent
user_reviews.Description: The owner was kind and offered a good recommendation. It was complex in its flavors and delicious. I was offered three levels of spiciness and chose the medium level. I enjoyed every bite and the lemon alcohol free mojito was a nice compliment, rimmed, without or mixed with accompanying salt.

title: Viet Burrito Danang
address: 29 Trần Quốc Toản, Hải Châu, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Camille Madronio
user_reviews.Description:
Vietnam Burrito looks like a grab-and-go spot—the kind you expect to be quick and transactional. That assumption didn’t quite hold. The food took a bit longer than expected, but it’s clear why: everything is made fresh and arrives properly hot.

We ordered the tofu burrito, black coffee, and lime juice. The coffee leaned slightly sweet likely pre-sweetened while the lime juice was well-balanced and refreshing.

The burrito itself was fabulous! It tasted clean and nourishing, the kind of meal that feels like it’s doing you a favor. What stood out most was the texture: there were crisp elements inside that gave it a satisfying crunch, adding a bit of bite and contrast that kept each mouthful interesting.

Seating downstairs is limited, but there’s a more spacious upstairs area with additional tables and chairs. A practical setup, even if the first impression suggests otherwise.

I am going to sum up like this: it's quite run down inside but the food is way too good to pass up. A hidden gem.

user_reviews.Name: Andre Bella
user_reviews.Description: Yesterday I went to another Mexican spot and got two sad, flavorless tacos with zero toppings, which left me completely disappointed. Today, I decided to try Viet Burrito, and it was a night-and-day difference! For about the exact same price (around 150,000 VND), the portions here are large, loaded, and incredibly flavorful. They actually give you a great, satisfying meal. Yesterday I was sad, but today Viet Burrito made me very happy! 😊 Highly recommended.

user_reviews.Name: Juan Gonzalez
user_reviews.Description:
As a tourist who has access to authentic casual Mexican food all the time, I had to see what Vietnamese people think Mexican food is like. I got their Regular Burrito and guacamole.
This wasn't the worst interpretation of Mexican food I've tried, but it it did leave much to be desired.
The majority of their patrons likely don't have much experience with Mexican food in their home countries, so it makes sense that they think the food here is great.
So at the end of the day, if you don't know any better, the food is fine, but coming from a real Mexican's perspective, the food here is pretty mid.

user_reviews.Name: Phill and Dat
user_reviews.Description:
The food was good, we had chicken quesadillas, a chicken burrito, salsa, and an order of tortilla chips. Both had ample amounts of chicken, and tasted good. What it lacked was an authentic Mexican taste, they were rather bland from a spice perspective. Disappointed that no sour cream with the quesadillas, mayo instead. The size of the dishes were great!

I ordered a side of Guacamole, and a side of chips.  The Guac was tasteless, no trace of lime, very bland. The chips (looked and tasted like fried wonton) were good, but again lacked a corn taste, not authentic.

To conclude, the food was good, just lacking Mexican spice. The staff and restaurant were great.

user_reviews.text_translated: This is my second time eating here. The food is delicious, the portions are generous, the meat is fresh, and most importantly, they have air conditioning! There are three air conditioners and fans, so it's not hot! I don't understand why restaurants in Vietnam don't like to turn on air conditioning or don't have it at all 🥲 It's so hot here, thankfully this place is cool enough.

user_reviews.Name: Lan Pham
user_reviews.Description:
This place is special. You must try.

Innovative, delectable 8-course tasting menu, or a la carte. Unique combination of flavors and textures, elegant plating. Global cuisine with homage to Vietnamese ingredients. Chef Curtis is a perfectionist and you can tell with each bite.

Attentive, friendly service from young staff. The proper timing of eight courses is complicated, yet they executed brilliantly. Presentation of courses was seamless and each was nicely explained in both Vietnamese, and the later courses in English, upon request. This was my second visit and props to them for mastering a new menu monthly.
Manageable but varied wine list and local craft beer. Great guidance on pairings.

Standout, upscale but welcoming frontage and interiors. Bar seats allowing front row to the culinary skill on the open-flame grill, or more intimate booth seating near the large picture window. Fun 80s pop music in the background.

An impressive feat for the talented (and handsome) chef and his crew to pull off this level of dining in Danang.

Excited for the next seasonal menu!

user_reviews.Name: Rosaline Jos
user_reviews.Description:
I visited Westwood once for dinner when the main chef/owner was present, and the experience was genuinely good. The food, service, and overall operation were smooth and well executed. That positive first impression is precisely why I decided to return, this time for lunch, together with my partner, who is also a chef.

Before coming, we made a reservation for two after reviewing both the set menu and the à la carte menu listed on Google. Upon arrival, however, the experience immediately felt different. There were two staff members in the open kitchen watching us rather uncomfortably, while another waiter stood aside using his phone.

We were then presented with a breakfast and lunch menu, which we did not find appealing. We showed the staff the à la carte menu displayed on their Google listing and politely asked if it was available. The waitress informed us that that menu was for dinner only. As we were not interested in the breakfast or lunch options, we decided to stay briefly for drinks before leaving.

Unfortunately, even this part was disappointing. When my partner asked whether they had a blonde beer, none of the staff could answer, and the response came across as dismissive, as if the question itself was unreasonable and we should simply choose from what was printed on the menu without asking for further details. I also asked about how the prawn–clam juice used in the Bloody Mary is made, and again, no one could provide an answer. Throughout this time, the kitchen staff were on their phones, despite the restaurant being completely empty apart from us.

This experience was particularly disheartening not because it was bad on a first visit, but because it followed a genuinely good one. Returning with expectations shaped by a positive prior experience, only to encounter such a lack of professionalism and engagement, felt like a slap in the face.

It is a real pity to see a restaurant with so much potential operate well only when the owner or manager is present. Consistency is fundamental in hospitality, and unfortunately, this visit showed a stark contrast to what Westwood is capable of delivering.

title: Trattoria da Stefano

user_reviews.Name: Mary Ogborn
user_reviews.Description: I was craving authentic Italian food and was so glad to discover this beautiful restaurant while walking around Da Nang. I tried the pasta alle vongole, which was incredibly fresh and delicious—reminded me of my time living in Napoli! I also enjoyed the fresh patatine fritte with pecorino, the tiramisù, and, of course, a caffè. Everything was amazing—the tiramisù was hands down the best I’ve ever had. The owners are so friendly and really great people. I’ll definitely be back. Grazie mille! ❤️

user_reviews.Name: Oleg Valyn
user_reviews.Description:
I’ll start by saying that I’m not a fan of Italian cuisine. My friends like it, so we came here for dinner.

We were among the first guests, and our table was set outside. The first thing that stands out is the owner’s attitude — very personal and welcoming, almost as if you’re visiting his home. That part is genuinely приятное and creates a strong first impression.

At the same time, I can’t objectively judge Italian food because it’s simply not my thing. For me, it feels too simple, heavy on carbs, and lacking the balance I personally look for.

My friends did enjoy it, though. I tried their pasta — for me, it was just pasta, nothing particularly impressive.

I ordered cod with potatoes. It was decent, but a bit bland.

As for drinks: the red wine, even the one served by the glass, was quite good. The white wine I didn’t like at all. The prosecco felt more like a generic sparkling alcoholic drink rather than something distinctive.

That said, the selection of Italian wines is good, and the prices are reasonable for Da Nang.

Overall: a cozy place on a small street in the center, with a welcoming owner, a nice atmosphere, and decent wine. The food — depends on your taste.

user_reviews.Name: Her Dolce Vita
user_reviews.Description:
Absolutely loved our visit to this really cute restaurant. We both ordered the Bún chả and it was honestly outstanding. The meat was incredibly tender and just melted in the mouth, full of flavour and cooked perfectly. You can tell a lot of care goes into the food here 🥰

Service was friendly and efficient, and everything arrived quickly while still feeling freshly prepared.

We will definitely be coming back and highly recommend this spot to anyone looking for delicious authentic food in a lovely setting.

user_reviews.Name: TM ONG
user_reviews.Description:
The both of us took a grab bike here about 12.30pm for lunch. There were indoor and outdoor seating both very well ventilated.

We ordered our food from the big hardcover menu which was very well done with clear information and good photos. Food took a while to be served even though the restaurant was well staffed. Overall the food was very well presented. Portions were adequate. Taste was hit and miss as we are not Vietnamese and cannot appreciate certain flavors. You can never go wrong with spring roll and bun cha.
Place was definitely more pricey than your local restaurant or corner joint. But it gives a good representation of Vietnamese food in a controlled hygienic environment.
Restaurant location is also just across the jetty where the boat leaves for river cruises

user_reviews.Description:
I can't believe how bad their food are.  I was there for lunch and order Soft Shell Crab spring roll, Pho and shrimp and meat spring roll.
Their soup base Pho it's almost taste like water basically no flavor at all, and the meat is hard to chew.
The shrimp meat spring roll was just edible.
Their soft shell crab, You can taste and smell the bad odor after you bite into it.

I just don't understand how Michelin rating will give them a consistent select destination.
People at Michelin should go and undercover and dress like a foreigner that's on tour. This way they will find out how bad they are.

title: LANG CUA Seafood

user_reviews.Name: JY Sung
user_reviews.Description:
The service was great with a decent atmosphere, but was a bit let down by the food when comparing it to other places.

Maybe it was because we visited quite late or expectations might’ve been too high, but dishes took a long time to arrive even though we were the only table and flavor was slightly lacking. Seafood was fresh though, so no issues there :).

Still an okay spot to grab a bite for the price if craving seafood, but would visit earlier in the day.

EDIT: Updated to 4 stars after considering the great service from the waitress.

user_reviews.Name: 许苏
user_reviews.Description: An unexpectedly delicious meal. The crabs were particularly tasty. I highly recommend the Singapore sauce. There was also someone playing the guitar on the spot. While enjoying the delicious seafood, one could listen to someone performing "Casablanca" live. Could there be a better experience than this?

user_reviews.Name: Viola Wong
user_reviews.Description: The food is nice overall, but it could be better. Maybe I had higher expectations before coming. I’ve visited other restaurants and tasted better food compared to this. The prawns were a bit small as well. Still an okay experience.

user_reviews.Name: merriment82
user_reviews.Description: Came on a Friday evening, it was empty. Most food on the menu was not available (example ramen, noodles). Ordered one crab, one steamed clam, prawns, grilled scallop, beef spaggo and white rice. Waited for a good 40+ mins for the main course to be served. Asked twice for the white rice before it was served. Steamed Clams were totally not depicted in the menu. Disappointing service.

user_reviews.Name: Gerald Lam
user_reviews.Description:
On a stormy night in June'25, sploshing thru road-rivers, we nearly missed the restaurant along the row of eateries and massage spas. We were the Only table there at 8pm on a Wednesday night.
Simple setup, clear menu with various 'combos' or ala carte.
We picked Crab in Buttergarlic, Lobster in half Buttergarlic and Coconut sauce, plain-steamed Prawns, and garlic fried rice.
All of them were offered as 'medium' size only, but i'ld say they were more like small+.
The tastes were good, especially the crustaceans in that creamy Buttergarlic sauce; each with a bun to dunk into. The coconut-cream rendition was interestingly different too. A let-down was the garlic fried rice which wasn't very fragrant, and had hard bits in it; probably garlic, maybe half-cooked rice.
But the price of D1.4m justified the meal, no complaints!

user_reviews.Description: We came in around 7:30pm on a Sunday night and there was only one other party. We were served quickly and service was attentive and great. title: Indus Indian Restaurant - Nhà hàng Ấn Độ - 인도 음식점
address: 02-04 An Thượng 26, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 556920, Vietnam
plus_code: 364V+2P Ngu Hanh Son, Da Nang, Vietnam

owner.name: Indus Indian Restaurant - Nhà hàng Ấn Độ - 인도 음식점 (Owner)

user_reviews.Name: Beverly Chen
user_reviews.Description:
This is our favorite Indian restaurant in Da Nang—we’ve been here like three times!
Hands down the best. The service is great, fast, and on top of it! The ambiance is awesome, with great music—no sad songs!
The food is outstanding and consistent, which is very important to me as a chef.
Also, the BEST naan—garlic or cheese garlic!
I 100000% recommend this place!

user_reviews.Name: Karina
user_reviews.Description: Best Indian food I've ever had in my whole life! I especially recommend cheese&garlic Naan, mutton (lamb) biryani and mutton vindaloo. I drank salted lassi that was excellent too. Everything was freshly prepared using high quality ingredients - the sauces were thick and creamy, the toppings proper and the lamb meat just melted in my mouth. I would like to come back. So lucky I discovered Indus at my 1st day here. The service was excellent, too!

user_reviews.Name: SARAH Hodgman
user_reviews.Description: Literally just stumbled upon this restaurant whilst out walking. What can we say, we have eaten in many Indian restaurants around the world, but it would be difficult to find anywhere better. The onion bhaji, delicious and freshly made. Our two curries, wonderful, with just the right amount of spice. The waiting staff are charming, patient and smiling. The only place in 5 weeks in Vietnam if anyone has asked if we are enjoying our food, in any cuisine. Highly recommended. Just unfortunate we found this place on our final evening.

user_reviews.Name: Aishwarya Shedge
user_reviews.Description:
We stayed in Da Nang for three nights, and since Indus  Restaurant was close to our stay, we ended up going there almost every day!

We tried their dosa, chicken biryani, chicken tikka, chicken kadhai, chicken noodles, and a refreshing orange-cinnamon drink — everything was absolutely delicious. Honestly, we didn’t expect the dosa to be that good!

The staff is super friendly, the vibe is great, and we were so busy enjoying the food that we forgot to take photos. 😄

Definitely a must-visit in Da Nang!

Amazing food, best Indian food we had in Vietnam so far. We ordered a lot of food, highly recommend the fish curry, mutton biryani, daal, samosa chaat, chilli chicken, dahi puri and the honey chilli chips.

user_reviews.Name: Stevie B
user_reviews.Description:
OMG 😆 Wife and I found our foodie happy place! Today we stopped by for dinner and the food is fantastic! The flavors just smack you in your mouth,  putting a smile on your face to last you for days. The staff... best service 👌 🙌 👏 ❤️ They were so kind and attentive, it was a though you were sitting in their home kitchen.
We had the following dishes
... Chicken Vindaloo
...  Basmati Rice
... Palak Paneer
... Tandoori Roti
... Mango 🥭 Lassi
This place is going to the top of my list.
You will see us again very soon.
Thank you for making us smile.  😃
We are in our foodie happy place.

user_reviews.Name: Devang Patel
user_reviews.Description: If you are craving authentic Indian food in Da Nang, Indus Indian Restaurant is a must-visit! Both the food and the service are absolutely amazing. We stopped in and ordered the  Cheeze Dosa for the kids, which was a huge hit. The Samosa Chaat was incredibly tasty and a great starter. The absolute highlight for me was the Chole Bhature—it genuinely nailed that authentic Delhi taste! We finished the meal with the Kheer, which was a good, sweet ending to a fantastic dinner. Highly recommend!

user_reviews.Description:
Beautiful Indian dinner along with the best service.

We shared the Dhai Puri for entree; then the Dal Tadka, along with rice and the Indus special Dosa.

Tiger beer and a salted Lassi (with jeera!)

Can’t ask for better service or find more authentic Indian food… served promptly too.

Thank you guys!! 🙏🙏

user_reviews.Name: Jenny J
user_reviews.Description: A beautifully decorated place with excellent food and excellent service. The food was not only artistically presented, it also tasted delicious. Every dish was made just right with fresh and rich flavor. I particularly liked the oysters 3 way, which I was told was new this year. One way was the typical plain oyster, but the other two had really interesting sauces. I really liked the 2nd way, a refreshing flavor compliments the raw oyster without overpowering. Just excellent. We have been to quite a few Michelin stared restaurants, this one is just as good in my opinion with larger portions and a more reasonable price. Highly recommended.

user_reviews.Name: Carmen wong
user_reviews.Description:
We had such a memorable dining experience thanks to Mr. Tai, our server. He is incredibly knowledgeable — able to explain every ingredient in each dish with confidence. Beyond that, his friendly personality and great sense of humor made the evening even more enjoyable.

What impressed us most was his thoughtfulness. Knowing we wanted to catch the Dragon Bridge firework show, Mr. Tai went the extra mile by helping us call a Grab and guiding us to the best spot to watch. That kind of care and attention truly elevated our experience.

Thank you, Mr. Tai, for making our evening special. Your professionalism and kindness left a lasting impression!

We also thanks to the Chef who compliments us a set of Appetizer.

We ordered 1 set and extra main course. The food is delicious and each dish is like a piece of art.

We really enjoy our dinner and had a great experience.

title: My Hanh Seafood
address: 03-05 Võ Nguyên Giáp, An Hải, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Rodelio Soriano (InRodWeTrust)
user_reviews.Description:
We celebrated our 7th wedding anniversary lunch at My Hanh Seafood and honestly, this place exceeded our expectations.

The seafood was absolutely fresh and delicious. We ordered fresh coconut juice together with several of their Michelin-recognized dishes, and every single one was worth it. The flavors, presentation, and quality were top-notch.

What made the experience even more memorable was the exceptional service from Huỳnh Trâm and Văn Tao. They were both incredibly attentive, warm, and genuinely helpful throughout our meal. Whenever they noticed us struggling with the seafood, they immediately stepped in to assist us with a smile. That level of customer care is rare and truly appreciated.

Another thoughtful touch was at the end of the meal — they provided a bowl with lime and water for hand washing to remove the fishy smell. Simple detail, but very classy and thoughtful.

The restaurant also has a beautiful seaside atmosphere that made our anniversary lunch even more special. Yes, it’s a bit pricey, but the food quality, service, and overall experience made it worth every penny. I honestly don’t mind paying for this kind of excellent dining experience.

Highly recommended if you’re visiting Da Nang and looking for premium seafood with outstanding hospitality. Special thanks again to Huỳnh Trâm and Văn Tao for making our anniversary celebration extra memorable.

Dear Valued Guest,

My Hanh Seafood would like to sincerely thank you for taking the time to share your kind feedback and leave us a 5-star review on Google.

Your trust and support are a great honor to us and a strong motivation for our team to continuously improve every aspect of the dining experience — from the quality of our dishes and the atmosphere to the level of service we provide.

user_reviews.Name: Felix Hung
user_reviews.Description:
We came here on our first night in Da Nang because of the beautiful view and the comfortable air-conditioned setting, but unfortunately the experience did not live up to the price.

The seafood was fresh, but the flavors were only average considering how expensive everything was. We ordered 5 dishes for our family and the bill came out to over $150 USD, which felt extremely overpriced compared to many other restaurants we visited afterward in Da Nang. Surprisingly, we even left still feeling hungry afterward, which made the price even harder to justify.

The bigger disappointment was the service. I completely understand that not everyone speaks English, and I would never expect that while traveling in another country. What stood out to us was the lack of patience or effort in communication. At many other restaurants in Vietnam, staff were kind, welcoming, and would even pull out Google Translate to help bridge the language barrier. Here, we often felt rushed or like our questions were an inconvenience.

We also had to repeatedly ask for basic things we had already requested — utensils, rice, and even the check. Multiple items took several reminders before they arrived, which made the dining experience frustrating instead of relaxing.

To be fair, the location and view are excellent, and the seafood itself seemed fresh. But overall, it felt more like a tourist trap than a restaurant focused on hospitality or value.

Ironically, our Grab driver warned us beforehand that this place was expensive and tourist-oriented, and I wish we had listened. We avoided smaller local spots because we wanted AC and convenience on our first night, but in hindsight there are many better-value restaurants in Da Nang with friendlier service and better overall experiences.

user_reviews.Name: Bach Nguyen
user_reviews.Description:
I have been a frequent customer since the previous owner was in charge and I have consistently had a wonderful experience over the years at Mỹ Hạnh. The quality of the seafood here is always excellent and incredibly fresh.

My recommendation for the most authentic experience is to skip the printed menu and go directly to the tanks to select your seafood and have it weighed.

On my most recent visit, the tôm sóc tỏi was a highlight because it was prepared with crispy rice, which provided a perfect crunch and unique texture. Additionally, the canh chua cá nâu is a classic dish that they execute perfectly; it is simple but incredibly flavorful and well-balanced. This restaurant remains a reliable and high-quality destination for anyone looking for authentic seafood in Da Nang.

Bonus get a table by the beach.

user_reviews.Name: Hassan Al-Ansari
user_reviews.Description:
I visited My Hanh Seafood today and it was a very pleasant experience from start to finish. The restaurant offers a wide variety of fresh Vietnamese seafood, and everything I tried was cooked with great flavor and balance. The ingredients felt fresh, the seasoning was authentic, and the taste truly reflects the local culinary style of Da Nang.

The atmosphere of the restaurant is relaxing, with a nice view and a comfortable dining area that adds positively to the overall experience. The staff were polite, attentive, and quick to assist whenever needed. Service was smooth, and the food arrived on time without unnecessary delays.
What I appreciated most was how well the dishes were prepared simple  clean, and full of natural seafood taste without overpowering sauces. It’s a great place for anyone who wants to enjoy Vietnamese-style seafood in a clean and organized setting.

Overall, My Hanh Seafood is worth visiting if you’re in Da Nang. Good food, good service, and a truly local seafood experience. I definitely recommend it and would be happy to come back again.

user_reviews.Description: Selection of seafood is excellent! Pham Thi Tham has been very hospitable in making our family dinner very special. Thank you for welcoming us with open arms. Just not our cup of tea when it comes to higher sugar content in cooking style. However, I would be open to coming back to try additional items off the menu IF we make it back to Danang . Nice job My Hanh team!

user_reviews.Name: Monica DeWane
user_reviews.Description:
The portions were huge! I'm very grateful to the server who deterred me from ordering an even larger crab. The food was good. I suggest if you're particular about the sauce or butter for your crab, you might ask more questions because I ordered garlic sauce thinking it was going to simply be garlic and it was very sweet.
Overall the prices are a little high high but to be expected for seafood in Da Nang. The ambiance seaside can't be much better.

user_reviews.Name: Mike O
user_reviews.Description:
Ok just like Mango Nguyen said in an order a few days ago, the seafood fried rice was just terrible.

First, I walked up at 430pm and 1 table was taken in the whole place, first I said I'd have a beer and they didn't want me to stay but I said an appetizer too and they let me in, oh thanks!

Order a pint which was cold I'll give them that. Patiently waited with menu down for a bit and finally ordered the seafood fried rice. Was now looking forward to a great pre dinner (dinner in a few hours already planned) for some yummy goodness.

It arrives, as you see in the picture it looked great!  I dug in expecting pure love and joy but it ended up with the most bland meal I've had, even white bread had more flavor.  No seasonings, shrimp wasn't stir fried and if steamed it was literally frozen food dry as hell, same for the calamari (maybe that's what it was), totally let down. Second picture is what I left after attempting to make this a better meal.

For a "Michelin mentioned" place, you expect all of their dishes are good but not at all.  I paid my bill and left, I'm not one to ask for discounts and would rather just let you all know to never order this at this place. Also, sorta rude to make people order when it's dead inside and everyone was avoiding to ask if I wanted another drink or if the food is ok. Maybe new management/ owner that is clueless, sorry do some spot checks on your place.

Going for some pho to wash down this disappointment.

title: CHAY corner in Danang- Vietnamese Dumplings
address: 17 Lê Hữu Trác, An Hải, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

owner.name: CHAY corner in Danang- Vietnamese Dumplings (Owner)

user_reviews.Name: Karishma Mehta
user_reviews.Description:
Came here reading so many good reviews for their vegan vietnamese cuisine, and i am so happy we got a chance to taste their cooking and warmth and love in their food. Na is the owner and she cooks along with her mother, such a homely warm vibe they give and everything is prepared fresh when you order. Their staff student Chi was also very sweet and caring.
We had the traditional dumplings, summer rolls, mango sushi, garlic rice, quang noodles and fried tofu in seaweed. Everything was exceptional. I suggest all vegetarians to definitely try this place when in Danang and they also have a branch in Hoi an.

user_reviews.Description:
Lo
vely restaurant experience! 🤩

The food was incredibly delicious — even though it’s vegetarian, it will satisfy any hardcore meat lover without a doubt. Everything was full of flavor and truly impressive.

The service was quick, prices were very fair, and the older lady serving us was just wonderful — you can really feel that she genuinely wants the best for her guests. Such a warm and authentic atmosphere!

I ordered the surprise combo and was more than happy with it — a great choice if you want to try something special.

Overall, a very authentic and unique place that stands out from the rest. I’ll definitely come back. Thank you and keep it up! 🫶🏻✨

user_reviews.Name: Missy Nguyen
user_reviews.Description: I was very disappointed in this place as the reviews were promising. Maybe I am alone in my opinion but I thought this place was incredibly mid. The dumplings are a lot smaller than the pictures. We got the 15 mixed dumplings and I honestly couldn’t tell the dumplings apart. The fillings were not much and all tasted the same. We also got the banh xeo to share and as you can tell from the photo there is not much filling. The two end pieces of the banh xeo were empty. Which would have been fine if the middle pieces had more filling. The “greens” for the banh xeo were also sad. And I had to ask for rice paper to wrap the banh xeo. I left this establishment very unsatisfied and confused as to why it was rated so high. It is also more expensive than nearby quan chay where you can get better food. Maybe this place is more appealing to the palate of foreigners than Vietnamese people. However, the people running this shop were quite lovely.

user_reviews.Name: Jia Moon
user_reviews.Description:
Hard to find vegan dumplings 🥟🌱🥟💚🥟

Many different flavors!
These are very small, like micro-dumplings
10 pieces for 30k dong

Very saucy
Tried the traditional (mixed) dumplings
Again, these dumplings are tiny.  Hard to appreciate the flavor of the filling because it is a very small amount in each dumpling.
You taste more the sweet soy sauce they are swimming in than anything inside.

The textures are varied...chewy (glutinous wrap) with fried crunchy wrapper skin bits.
👍
It was fun to try
But I would not eat the dumplings again.

I much prefer the chubby Chinese style dumplings that take 2-3 bites to finish

But nice to have vegan dumpling options
🥟💚🌱🥟💚🌱🥟

Only feedback would be that the tapioca skin feels a bit too thick, that can be thinner a little and filling qty can be increased. But thats my personal opinion. I would surely recommend this place to my vegetarian friends.

title: Saffron By The Beach - Indian Restaurant Da Nang
address: 100 Võ Nguyên Giáp, An Hải, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

owner.name: Saffron By The Beach - Indian Restaurant Da Nang (Owner)

user_reviews.Name: Kirti Dhingra
user_reviews.Description: Had a great experience at Saffron by the Beach. As vegetarians, it can be difficult to find really good food in many Asian countries where seafood is the main focus, so this place was a pleasant surprise. The food was delicious and we really enjoyed it. It is a bit on the expensive side though, we paid around 880,000 VND for two people. Overall, definitely worth visiting if you’re looking for good vegetarian-friendly food.

user_reviews.Name: Peter Altholz
user_reviews.Description: We're staying at the hotel next door (Marriott Villas) and decided to walk down the beach for dinner at Saffron. So glad we did! We've been in the Da Nang area for 3+ weeks now (bouncing between Da Nang city and Hoi An) and this is the BEST Indian food we have had. All of the curries were super flavorful. Nan was slightly crispy and delicious. Overall, great meal and attentive service. We can't wait to be back. (And p.s. unlike many restaurants in Da Nang, nobody asked us to write a review, this place is just so good I feel the need to spread the word)

Knowing that our food stood out as the best Indian meal you’ve had during your time in Da Nang and Hoi An means a lot to our team. Your kind words and genuine recommendation are deeply appreciated, and we look forward to welcoming you back again very soon.

user_reviews.Name: Piyush Menghani
user_reviews.Description:
If you’re craving authentic Indian food, this place is an absolute must-visit. Every dish is packed with rich, vibrant flavors that feel both traditional and perfectly balanced. From the aromatic spices to the freshness of the ingredients, you can really taste the quality in every bite.

What truly sets this restaurant apart is not just the food, but the exceptional service. The staff is warm, attentive, and genuinely cares about your experience, making you feel welcome from the moment you walk in.

Great Indian food, incredible flavor, and top-notch service — it’s a combination that’s hard to beat. Highly recommended!

user_reviews.Name: Sharvani Metekar
user_reviews.Description: Really bad place. The food does not have a good taste despite having such high prices. Not at all worth it because the menu card prices are too expensive and if they have expensive prices the food should be worthy enough to eat. But sadly it's not, and the service is too slow. Despite having such a moderate amount of staff the people don't take enough efforts to serve quickly. For one order we need to atleast wait for 15-20 mins and even the bill is not given on time. Staff especially the owner is really careless. If the staff is busy atleast the owner could give us the bill. I would recommend their are much more indian restaurants in Danang which are really worth the try. If you want you can visit this restaurant but I myself with personal experience do not recommend this restaurant.

user_reviews.Name: Thang Segerberg
user_reviews.Description: Very nice and attentive staff. They could speak very good english – very heplful. The food was amazing. High quality, flavourful, sumptuous – in a good way – and simply delicious. The atmosphere was very calm and relaxed with soothing indian music playing in the background. There are also many windows which allows you to have a nice view while you eat – I really recommend choosing one of the tables close to the ocean. All in all the restaurant was top notch. Really recommend!

title: D1 Vietnamese Restaurant 베트남 식당

user_reviews.Name: momorrr
user_reviews.Description:
The bun cha was perfectly seasoned — absolutely delicious.
I had been curious about the mango salad, and it was fresh and flavorful as well. We ended up having a really great lunch.

The ingredients tasted very fresh, and the overall seasoning was spot on.
We were thinking of ordering more dishes, but the staff kindly let us know that it might be too much food, which honestly saved us. We really appreciated that thoughtful advice.

The woman at the restaurant was also lovely and very pleasant, which made the experience even nicer.

And one more thing — whether they belong to the restaurant or a nearby shop, there were calm, adorable parent-and-child dogs relaxing around our feet. It was such a peaceful and heartwarming moment.

Great food, a warm atmosphere, and a very enjoyable lunch.
I would happily come back.

user_reviews.Description:
D1 is our number one favorite place to eat in Da Nang!  We get the Bun Thit Nuong Cha Gio every time, because it is the best food we have ever put in our faces!

user_reviews.Name: marco mesina
user_reviews.Description:
D1 is one of my favorite restaurants in Da Nang. Located on Antuong 5, it’s a true gem of Vietnamese cuisine. The dishes are always fresh, traditional, and prepared with great attention to detail.

What I appreciate the most is their flexibility: I often ask for dishes with fewer carbohydrates or completely without carbs, and the team always accommodates my requests with kindness. It’s rare to find a restaurant that personalizes dishes so well while maintaining authentic Vietnamese flavors.

The restaurant is small, but extremely clean and welcoming, and the service is excellent—professional, fast, and always very friendly.

I highly recommend D1 to anyone who wants to enjoy genuine Vietnamese food, prepared with love and served with outstanding hospitality.

user_reviews.Name: Sergejs Ponomarjovs
user_reviews.Description:
Bếp Bà Ba

Very authentic place with a lot of small, interesting interior details. Feels like a real Vietnamese home-style restaurant, not staged or touristy. Spacious, clean, and comfortable.

Menu is wide, lots of choices, so it works well if you’re coming with more than one person and everyone wants something different.

People here are friendly and welcoming. Nice extra touch: we were served a free welcome drink, and after the meal we got a free dessert — watermelon. Simple, but very appreciated.

Portions are good, food quality is solid, everything tasted fresh and properly cooked. Prices are low, especially for the location near the beach.

Overall, a very pleasant experience: authentic atmosphere, good food, friendly service, and fair pricing. A place I’d happily return to.

Ignore the idiot last week who reviewed this place as pub street.  Irrelevant to the food and service in this restaurant.

user_reviews.Name: person work
user_reviews.Description:
There aren't even any waiters around, they're busy with other things. I'm sitting here waiting for the bill.
The soup arrived. In the photo, there's a piece of fish, a nice center section. In reality, they only included the head and tail. I guess they wanted to make sure there were enough bones. There's practically nothing to eat there, and the flaxseed isn't very rich.

They didn't even charge me for it. Of course, it raised my rating at Plis. But the fact that I sat there for so long and didn't eat properly isn't very good.

user_reviews.Name: Walter Barning
user_reviews.Description:
Lovely Vegan is hands down the best restaurant I’ve been to in Vietnam, and honestly one of the best I’ve visited in years. Every dish is a surprise, full of flavor, creativity, and incredibly fresh vegetables. Even something as simple as ginger tea feels refined and special here.
The staff deserves a special mention: super friendly, genuinely welcoming, and highly professional. You feel taken care of from the moment you walk in.
On top of all that, the prices are unbelievably reasonable for the quality you get. They absolutely nailed everything. A must-visit in Da Nang

user_reviews.Name: Camille Madronio
user_reviews.Description:
It’s rare to see a vegan spot this busy, so curiosity did the work for us. From the outside, it looks more like a garage than a restaurant but surprisingly airy, well-ventilated, and packed without the chaos of a queue. Good sign.

The menu is broad and reasonably priced. We went for the pho, burrito, protein spring bowl - a house special, and a lemonade with salted dried apricot.

The pho arrived warm within seconds, with mushrooms doing a convincing impression of beef. The broth carries it: clean, savory, and deeply comforting without trying too hard.

The protein bowl leans wholesome with quinoa, while the burrito is essentially the same cast wrapped in a tortilla, different format, same satisfaction. Both come with a rich eggplant sauce.

The drink was serviceable, slightly bland, but it clears the palate well enough after the meal.

Overall: an excellent vegan go-to with enough variety that you almost forget you’re eating “healthy food"
user_reviews.text_original:
It’s rare to see a vegan spot this busy, so curiosity did the work for us. From the outside, it looks more like a garage than a restaurant but surprisingly airy, well-ventilated, and packed without the chaos of a queue. Good sign.

The menu is broad and reasonably priced. We went for the pho, burrito, protein spring bowl - a house special, and a lemonade with salted dried apricot.

The pho arrived warm within seconds, with mushrooms doing a convincing impression of beef. The broth carries it: clean, savory, and deeply comforting without trying too hard.

The protein bowl leans wholesome with quinoa, while the burrito is essentially the same cast wrapped in a tortilla, different format, same satisfaction. Both come with a rich eggplant sauce.

The drink was serviceable, slightly bland, but it clears the palate well enough after the meal.

Overall: an excellent vegan go-to with enough variety that you almost forget you’re eating “healthy food"

user_reviews.Name: ivanas_tastykitchen
user_reviews.Description:
We’ve been here several times and it’s always consistently good. Great spot not far from the beach—perfect for lunch before heading out, and we’ve even come back for dinner.

There’s a wide selection of food, but the Asian dishes are definitely the highlight and a must-try. The Western options are decent but a bit pricier for what you get.

Overall, a reliable and enjoyable place we keep coming back to.

title: Dhaba 28

plus_code: 26XW+HM Ngu Hanh Son, Da Nang, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Lisa G
user_reviews.Description: Great place! I was so excited to get my food that I forgot to take a pic. We ordered the madras chicken, Dhaba Sp Daal, jeera rice and garlic butter naan. All delicious and it was the perfect amount. Only thing better than the food was the service! Will definitely come back. 🤗
user_reviews.text_original: Great place! I was so excited to get my food that I forgot to take a pic. We ordered the madras chicken, Dhaba Sp Daal, jeera rice and garlic butter naan. user_reviews.reply_text_original:
Thank you so much for this wonderful and energetic review! We absolutely love the fact that you were too excited for the food to take a picture—that is the ultimate compliment for our kitchen!

We are thrilled that your order of Madras Chicken, our signature Dhaba Special Dal, Jeera Rice, and Garlic Butter Naan hit the perfect spot and provided the ideal balance for your meal. Hearing that our service was even better than the delicious food means the world to our front-of-house team, who work incredibly hard to make everyone feel at home.

user_reviews.Name: Oleg Valyn
user_reviews.Description:
The food is very delicious. We tried several dishes, and every dish was prepared very well, including two types of rice. Every grain of rice stood out with its fluffiness and richness of flavor. The meat was simply top-tier — the best Indian-style meat I’ve ever eaten. The samosa was also excellent. The lassi was a bit sweet for me, but overall very tasty. And of course, the Indian tea, after which I couldn’t sleep the entire night. Everything was extremely good.

The average bill for two came out to about 30–35 dollars. This food is definitely worth trying. Very delicious.

user_reviews.Name: Jai Bisht
user_reviews.Description:
I visited Dhaba 28 with high expectations, but unfortunately, the experience was quite disappointing from both a service and culinary perspective. Price and protion size good. But As someone who works in the hospitality industry, I feel it is important to provide this feedback constructively. Upon arrival, the restaurant was not busy (only two other tables), yet we were not greeted or welcomed. We had to find our own seats. So i thought it's normal her but Interestingly, we noticed later arrivals were greeted warmly and offered complementary water without asking, which we never received. This inconsistency in treatment is quite unprofessional. We also pay the same as per we order. We not demand any free or discount so this is very unfair to us.  Furthermore, the senior Indian staff (presumably management) remained behind the cash counter Gossip and laughing rather than overseeing the floor or ensuring guests were being looked after. There seemed to be a significant lack of training for the indian staff and Vietnamese staff also who struggled with the menu and order taking. Lack of Service Sequence The Aloo Paratha arrived first as a starter instead of paneer 65.In a professional kitchen, the tandoor and curry sections should coordinate so the meal flows correctly. The paratha was okay, but the curd was not whisked and it lacked the traditional pickle. Paneer 65 This lacked the essential South Indian "tadka" (curry leaves, mustard seeds, lemon wedge and black pepper). It felt like paneer coated in cornflour chill salt and food coloring—not an authentic representation. Chicken Biryani This was the biggest disappointment. It appeared to be white rice tossed in masala rather than a true biryani. Rice are very plain they not observe whole spices flavor and The overpowering scent of Kewra and rose water was used to mask and cover all other aroma a lack of depth in flavor. The chicken was soft it's boiled not cooked with biryani so lacked seasoning. Schezwan Fried Rice We explicitly ordered noodles, but received rice. I sure i ordered noodles and the guy also repeat it but don't whose mistake but The dish was heavily reliant on MSG and lacked the signature numbing heat of Sichuan peppercorns.

While the ambiance is nice and the orange juice was fresh, the food and service standards need urgent improvement. When representing Indian culture in abroad, the authenticity of the recipes and the warmth of the hospitality are key.
As i read many review and there replys so i need to make things very clear that i had my meal completely not wasted even wrong order(fried rice)  also and if you ask why not report to staff so i don't think it's make worth indian guy was busy in gossip and vietnamese can't even know the description of food. I am writing this not for a discount or free, but as a genuine professional critique. Also i am not saying this is home kitchen i know you hired professional but need really good improvement. I hope the management takes these suggestions to improve the training of their staff and the quality of their kitchen output. I would love to return in the future if these areas are addressed.

Update: I appreciate the apology included in the response, however I am disappointed by the overall tone and the personal remarks made about me rather than focusing on the feedback itself.

My review was written respectfully and based on my genuine experience on that day. It was not intended to offend, demand compensation, or question anyone’s profession. Constructive feedback should be met with professionalism, not sarcasm or assumptions about a guest’s background, taste, or other reviews.

I stand by my original review. The purpose was to highlight areas for improvement in service flow, communication, and food consistency. I truly hope the management takes this feedback in a positive spirit and uses it to grow.

Thank you

user_reviews.reply_text_original:
Mr Bisht,
Went through your review, more as an audit review., and is appreciated. 

Well we would like to sincerely apologise for the lapse of service regarding meet and assist and for the water not offered, apologies once again.  It should have been done without fail which is a norm in ANY restaurant. 

Regarding as you mentioned the restaurant had 2 tables , and we have a free seating policy where guests can choose their seating. And since you came for the first time so there was nothing personal where the services was not offered you intentionally. There are many guests who do return to us regularly and we do in human nature tend to meet them in a more warm way as we Indians are known for. 

Regarding the Indian staff, he has the required team to look after the guests, and going through the footage after ur detailed review want to tell you , is that the Vietnamese staff who has served you KNOWS HIS INDIAN FOOD MENU PRETTY CLEARLY, and has helped us to build the strong foundation of Dhaba 28 from day we started. GIVEN THE BENEFIT OF DOUBT ON PRONUNCIATION , chicken schezwan rice I/O noodles , , even before you paid the bill for chicken schezwan rice we would have really appreciated when food was served to clearly tell us about the  order flip. It would and could have been changed immediately. 

Regarding your taste and your knowledge, we are impressed but don’t wand to elaborate more on that 
We do feel you should know as you mentioned correctly  this not a home kitchen , and the chefs at DHABA 28 do tend to have some more in- depth knowledge in  food preparations  for restaurant rather than following a menu book.  Also not forgetting that certain ingredients, in an another country may not be available, so have to substitute. 

For the food flow your point is well taken into consideration and we will review why it happened. 

We analysed the video and wondering with many issues, you didn’t let us know about anything at the service stage or final bill settlement. If mentioned definitely would had been taken care of.

Since you mentioned you came to the restaurant reading the review, which unfortunately like a few other restaurants are not paid for we will try our best with your audit review to change a few things around . 

Seeing your  dissatisfied review about other restaurants too, sorry Dhaba 28  also could not serve as per your taste palette. I have to say on a very professional level reading ur feedbacks on certain restaurants that I may need a GORDON RAMSEY to get even 3 stars from you. 

Just out of curiosity , you own a culinary renting company where we met.

Oops nearly forgot in healthy conversation that step into dhaba 28 is a wheel chair ramp

user_reviews.Name: Matrice300
user_reviews.Description:
I was looking for something a little different from the local cuisine and thought I’d give this German restaurant a try based off of the reviews.
I was welcomed with warmth by a staff member and then spoke with the owners, whom are all very lovely genuine people, then sat down to a very delicious meal.
From the steak, schnitzel, German sausage, garlic bread to the biggest freshest salad I’ve had in a long time, my compliments go out to the chef.
I had returned for a second time before writing this review, and had the exact same experience upon arrival, the warm welcome, quality of food and great conversation.
Great value for money, will definitely return again.

Thank you all.

user_reviews.Name: văn tùng nguyễn
user_reviews.Description:
A wonderful experience at AnnHome – German Restaurant in Da Nang!
Authentic imported German beer and traditional German cuisine – from sausages to salted pork knuckle, everything was delicious and high quality. The atmosphere is cozy and friendly, making you feel like you’re in a small local spot somewhere in Germany.
Staff were attentive, fast, and very welcoming.
Perfect place to enjoy great food and drinks with friends or family – warm, sociable, and relaxed.
Definitely coming back again!

Der Besitzer spricht perfektes deutsch. The personell also speaks good english. They are nice and work fast.

Everyone going inside takes off their shoes, you might want to wear a good pair of socks.

The food was pretty good, the beef was super good and even the fries were good (and i usually dont like fries). The sauce of thr pasta was a bit watery, the owner explained thats because they make it from fresh tomato, but well good tomato sauce takes time if you make it fresh, usually a couple of hours. The spagetthi were al dente (at least at first, they cooked on in the watery sauce). The chilli that you could put inside made for a good and interesting taste.

The atmosphere is some of the better you find bit nothing noteworthy.

All in all of you want great beef or just want to go there for aome western food, you can kot go wrong by going there.

title: Gujarat Indian Restaurant Da nang
address: 39-41 Đỗ Bá, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam
plus_code: 362W+H7 Ngu Hanh Son, Da Nang, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Suman
user_reviews.Description:
I tried Chinese food and Gujarati Thali at Gujarat Indian Restaurant in Da Nang, Vietnam, and the experience was really good! 🇻🇳🇮🇳
The Gujarati thali was flavorful, well-balanced, and tasted authentic, with a nice variety of dishes. I also tried their Chinese food, which was surprisingly delicious and well-prepared. Every dish had good taste and proper spices.
The staff was very polite, friendly, and welcoming, which made the overall dining experience even better. Service was quick, and they were helpful with menu suggestions.
One of the best things about this restaurant is that the prices are very affordable, especially considering the quality and portion size. If you’re an Indian traveler, vegetarian, or someone craving Indian or Chinese food in Da Nang, this place is definitely worth visiting.
📍 Restaurant: Gujarat Indian Restaurant
📍 Location: Da Nang, Vietnam
🍽️ Cuisine: Indian (Gujarati Thali) & Chinese
💰 Price: Affordable
😊 Service: Friendly staff & good hospitality
If you’re traveling to Da Nang and looking for good Indian food, don’t miss this spot!

user_reviews.Name: Rajendra Giri
user_reviews.Description:
Visited Gujarat Indian Restaurant recently. The location is good and it’s close to the beach, which makes it convenient and pleasant. The restaurant looked clean and well maintained.

I was a bit surprised that it was empty around 6:30–7:00 PM and I was the only customer, which felt unusual for dinner time.

The food was not bad, but not excellent either—more of an average taste overall. Service was okay, nothing special but acceptable.

One thing they should really improve is the dining experience by upgrading their plates from plastic to at least ceramic plates, which would make the overall experience feel much better.

user_reviews.Name: Pratik Choudhari
user_reviews.Description:
Hands down the best veg Indian restaurant in Da Nang. We went (multiple days, both meals) as a family of 5, all adults. Every dish we ordered satisfied our cravings. The prices seem reasonable. Service is fast. Even with the restaurant at its capacity, we got our order of 8 items within 20mins.

Dal was really good. Same goes for paneer angara, black chana, masala and sev tomato. Their cold coffee is just like the one we get in India. Lassi was super super rich in flavour.

Just to try out how they make their breads, we ordered one of each kind, none of them disappointed us. Special shout out to fulkas.

The waiters were attentive and quick in their service. A lady (which i assume was the restaurant owner) asked us where are we from, how was the food and introduced herself, which I think was really nice.

user_reviews.Description:
The atmosphere, the food was really nice. I think it is the best Indian food restaurant in Da nang, Vietnam. The daal menu specifically, we really liked. We are a family of four with two kids. It was really nice having food in Gujrat da nang, and for service specific please ask for Alia. She's a real sweetheart and takes care of her orders really well.

We had dinner for two nights and breakfast for one day. Chai was also good.
