user_reviews.Name: Travelling Doctor
user_reviews.Description:
A Taste of Home in Da Nang: Gujarat Indian Restaurant
Finding authentic Indian flavors while traveling can be a hit or miss, but Gujarat Indian Restaurant in Da Nang is a definite "hit." Located conveniently on Do Ba Street, just a short walk from My Khe Beach, this place is a sanctuary for anyone craving high-quality, authentic Indian cuisine—especially vegetarians.
The Food: Pure Authentic Flavors
What sets this restaurant apart is its commitment to authenticity. Unlike many places that generalize "Indian food," Gujarat Indian Restaurant stays true to its roots while offering a broad menu that covers both North and South Indian specialties.
Vegetarian & Jain Haven: As a 100% vegetarian establishment, they truly understand the nuances of the diet. They even offer excellent Jain options, which can be hard to find in Vietnam.
Menu Highlights: The Thalis (platters) are the way to go for a complete meal. The Gujarati Thali and Punjabi Thali offer a perfect variety of curries, dal, and freshly made rotis.
Signature Dishes: Don't miss the Paneer Tikka Masala or their range of Khichdi (the Kathiyawadi Khichdi is particularly comforting). The homemade paneer is soft and flavorful, and the spices are balanced perfectly—not just "hot," but deeply aromatic.
Atmosphere and Service
The restaurant is spacious and clean, with a warm, inviting decor that reflects Indian cultural elements. It feels like a proper sit-down restaurant where you can relax and take your time.
Warm Hospitality: The service is standout. The staff (including the manager and chefs) are incredibly welcoming and often go out of their way to ensure the food is prepared to your specific spice preference. You can tell they take pride in their hospitality.
The "Home" Factor: There’s a reason you’ll often see large groups of Indian tourists and expats here—it’s the closest thing to a home-cooked meal you’ll find in the city.
Location and Practical Tips
Proximity to the Beach: It’s located in the vibrant An Thuong area, making it an easy stop after a morning at the beach or before a night out.
Pricing: While slightly higher than local Vietnamese street food, it is very reasonable for the quality of ingredients (many of which are imported directly from India) and the portion sizes.
Timing: They are open from 8:00 AM to 11:00 PM, making it a reliable spot for everything from a hearty breakfast to a late-night dinner.
Final Verdict
For anyone in Da Nang—whether you're a lifelong fan of Indian food or a curious traveler—Gujarat Indian Restaurant is a must-visit. It’s consistent, delicious, and serves as a wonderful cultural bridge in the heart of Vietnam. I’ll definitely be back for another Thali!

user_reviews.Name: A Logical IndiaN
user_reviews.Description:
IF GIVEN AN OPTION, I WOULD RATE THIS PLACE A MINUS 1 STAR!

Food was OK at best, but the service completely ruined our dining experience.

The plates arrived as soon as we got seated WITHOUT the spoons. Then the food arrived WITHOUT the serving spoon. We had to ask the server to please bring the serving spoon.
We ordered the tawa biryani, which comes with yogurt on the side. As soon as the dish arrived, we requested an extra serving of dahi so we could start eating properly. Unfortunately, that simple request was ignored—despite asking the staff FOUR separate times. The yogurt never arrived, and we were left waiting with our food getting cold.

The overall service was extremely disappointing. Our table wasn’t clean when we were seated, and there were no napkins or serviettes provided. Basic attention to detail was clearly missing.

What’s frustrating is that yogurt is something that’s already prepared—it requires almost no effort to serve. Yet even that seemed too much for the staff. The manager appeared more focused on taking orders rather than ensuring customers were actually being served. With only about three staff members handling roughly 50 guests, the place was clearly understaffed and poorly managed.

If you’re looking for a place with good service to match your meal, SKIP THIS PLACE!! .

user_reviews.Name: Candy S
user_reviews.Description: Waited a long time for a waiter to come take our order. It was only 2 people and we both wanted the same thing. How difficult is that? user_reviews.Name: Vicky Somaiya
user_reviews.Description:
The food was good. When you come to a foreign country and get food that tastes like home, nothing feels better than that.

However, the staff was very rude. We had ordered dal makhani and two rotis, and also dal khichdi. Later, he brought an extra roti. When we asked why he brought an extra roti, he said that we had ordered it. We clearly told him that we did not order it, but he still insisted and said it could not be cancelled. His behaviour was very rude and unprofessional.

Overall, it was not a good experience. In India, we say “Atithi Devo Bhava” (Guest is God), but here, abroad, they were arguing over just one roti. Very shameful behaviour from a Gujarat restaurant.

title: Nazaare- Indian beach restaurant and bar
address: Lô 02 Võ Nguyên Giáp, An Hải, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

owner.name: Nazaare- Indian beach restaurant and bar (Owner)

user_reviews.Name: Dr. Matt Jacobs
user_reviews.Description:
We visited NAZARE – Indian Beach Restaurant & Bar at night, and the setting itself made a great first impression. The beachside location gives the place a relaxed, breezy vibe, and in the evening it feels especially nice — calm, well lit, and perfect for a slow dinner by the sea.

We ordered prawn salad, chicken tikka, butter naan, and a butter chicken combo. Overall, the food was very good. The chicken tikka was well cooked and flavorful, the butter chicken was rich and comforting, and the butter naan paired well with it. The prawn salad was fresh and nicely done, a good lighter option to balance the meal.

The drinks were superb and honestly one of the highlights of the evening — well made, refreshing, and perfect for the beachside setting.

The only item that didn’t quite work for us was the chicken dosa. It wasn’t bad, but it didn’t match the standard of the other dishes and felt a bit underwhelming compared to the rest of the meal.

Service was friendly and attentive, and the staff were comfortable handling spice preferences and explaining dishes. The overall atmosphere — Indian food, good drinks, and a beach view at night — made for a very enjoyable experience.

Despite the one miss with the dosa, NAZARE is a place I’d still recommend if you’re in Da Nang and craving Indian food in a relaxed, scenic setting. Great vibe, solid food, excellent drinks — definitely worth a visit.

user_reviews.Name: Sarvesh Dhondge
user_reviews.Description:
We randomly came across this place while walking around the beach area in Da Nang, and honestly it turned out to be such a great find. We didn’t even realize at first that it was facing the beach, which made it even better.

It’s an Indian restaurant and felt like a total relief because we were really hungry and kind of done with Vietnamese food at that moment. We ordered chana masala with roti and jeera rice, and we absolutely loved it. The taste was on point and felt like proper comfort food.

We also met Mr. Dipankar who served us and gave some really good recommendations. Plus speaking in Hindi with someone here was actually fun :P

The place itself has a great vibe, right by the beach, and there was even a DJ and a stage which made it a nice spot to just sit, eat, and enjoy the atmosphere.

Definitely worth trying if you are around.

We had a wonderful time at Nazare.The restaurant has a very elaborate menu covering both south indian and north indian food. The location is superb and you can enjoy the beach vibe with great Indian food.

The service is also excellent and Pratik was prompt and professional.

user_reviews.Name: Terraveller
user_reviews.Description:
Irfan bhai maintains his food quality standards wherever he establishes his restaurant across Vietnam. His chain of Mazi is my favourite no brainer go to place while my stays at Hanoi. As a full time traveller, I usually prefer to eat regional food but once a while I wanna have a comfort food from back home and mazi / nazare never disappoints..

Pratik at Nazare was a great help.
The performances were amazing. The location is full 5 stars

user_reviews.Name: Sai Patnaik
user_reviews.Description:
Nazare Indian Restaurant, Da Nang
Dining at Nazare Indian Restaurant was truly a delightful experience. The restaurant offers a warm and inviting ambience, perfectly blending comfort with authentic Indian charm. The menu features a wide range of traditional Indian dishes, each prepared with rich flavours and aromatic spices that reflect true culinary craftsmanship. The service was attentive and courteous, making the entire dining experience seamless and enjoyable. The cleanliness, presentation, and consistency in taste truly make Nazare one of the finest Indian restaurants in Da Nang. Highly recommended for anyone craving authentic Indian cuisine in a refined setting.

user_reviews.Name: Devanté Benjamin
user_reviews.Description:
This place has a lot of potential but falls short in a few areas.

When you order papads, you only get one, which feels odd given the amount of chutney served alongside. It ends up being poor value compared to how it’s usually served as a shared starter.

The tandoori prawns looked great and the flavours were nice, but were slightly overcooked and a bit chewy.

The atmosphere was also affected by an excessively loud DJ set that didn’t match the dining experience. It felt more like an EDM rave than a restaurant.

That said, the Malvani fish curry was excellent, and the service was great, especially from Akash Singh who was very welcoming.

The restrooms could do with better upkeep, there were no paper towels.

user_reviews.Name: The Candid Critic
user_reviews.Description:
Nazare in Da Nang has a great location and a very spacious, well decorated ambience. The place is beautifully set up with lanterns, and they offer both open seating and ground style seating closer to the beach, which gives you a nice beach view. We made a reservation for the 31st night and were lucky to get a 9 pm slot, which worked out perfectly.

We ordered corn bhel, which was good, and chicken keema fries. The keema fries, however, were extremely oily and not something I enjoyed. We also ordered drinks, red wine and a gin and tonic. Later, we tried the prawn salad, which was really nice. For us, the main reason to be there was to enjoy dinner by the beach and catch the New Year’s fireworks, and for that, the place delivered.

Overall, Nazare is a nice spot for a romantic dinner in Da Nang, especially if you’re craving Indian food. You can enjoy drinks like beer or cocktails, the service is good, and the ambience really stands out. Definitely a recommended place for Indian cuisine with a great setting.

title: Cơm Nhà Vui

user_reviews.Name: Sergejs Ponomarjovs
user_reviews.Description:
Cơm Nhà Vui – local gem for Vietnamese food.

Amazing traditional Vietnamese dishes with a clear and easy-to-understand menu. The food is delicious, clean, and served fast. Service is super friendly – you really feel welcome here.

Loved the interior with old-school electronics – very unique and nostalgic vibe.

Highlights:
•	Great authentic Vietnamese food
•	Fast and friendly service
•	Excellent location
•	Ultra low prices
•	Personal favorite: stir-fry beef – outstanding
•	Nice personal touch: free dessert

Good to know:
Interior feels a bit worn and warm, but it didn’t bother us at all.

Overall:
Highly recommended if you want simple, tasty, and very affordable Vietnamese cuisine. A real local spot.

title: The Square Restaurant
address: 36 Bạch Đằng, Street, Hải Châu, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Marco Mercurio
user_reviews.Description:
Last night’s dinner at The Square Da Nang was nothing short of exceptional, and I genuinely mean that from start to finish.

From the moment we arrived, the tone was set by our hostess Anh, who was an absolute standout. Warm, attentive, and genuinely passionate about making sure every detail was perfect — she remembered preferences, checked in at just the right moments without ever feeling intrusive, and carried herself with a grace that elevated the whole experience. Anh is the kind of person who makes you feel like a valued guest rather than just a table number. She’s a real asset to this restaurant.

Now, the food. The set menu was a beautifully curated journey. We opened with Assorted Bread and Butter — simple but done properly, setting a confident tone. The Rémoulade Giant Crab Cake with microgreens, roasted bell pepper and spicy rémoulade sauce was a real highlight of the evening — generous, fresh, and that sauce had a lovely kick without overpowering the delicate crab.

The Wild Forest Mushroom Soup that followed was rich and deeply earthy, exactly what a good mushroom soup should be — silky and comforting with real depth of flavour.

The star of the show was undoubtedly the Premier Australian Beef Tenderloin Sous Vide — cooked to absolute perfection, melt-in-the-mouth tender, paired beautifully with gold potato, peppery arugula and a luxurious truffle jus. Proper fine dining execution.
We finished on a high with the Monalisa Gateau — raspberry, vanilla and almond — a light, elegant dessert that rounded off the meal without leaving you feeling heavy.

The only minor gripe of the evening was the seating arrangement. Despite the restaurant having around 50 empty tables, we were seated directly next to another couple with very little space between us. For a venue of this calibre, that felt like a missed opportunity to offer guests a more intimate and comfortable dining experience — especially on what felt like a quiet night. A small thing, but worth mentioning.

Overall though, The Square delivers on almost every level — refined cuisine, a beautiful setting, and staff who genuinely care. If you’re in Da Nang and want a special night out, this is the place. And ask for Anh — you won’t regret it.

user_reviews.Name: SaxyBites
user_reviews.Description:
Loved the breakfast buffet at The Square, Novotel Danang. There was such a good variety — from comforting local Vietnamese dishes to international favourites — plus a generous healthy corner. Honestly, it’s one of the best breakfast spreads I’ve had at an Accor hotel.

The view of the Han River from the restaurant is beautiful, especially in the morning. My only small suggestion would be to tint the windows a little more; the morning sun can get quite bright, and the glare makes it hard to enjoy the view. Not a big issue, just something that would make the experience even nicer.

The service was wonderful. Every staff member we met was warm, friendly, and incredibly quick to attend to anything we needed. The restaurant was also very clean and well-kept.

Kudos to the whole team — you made our morning a really pleasant one!

user_reviews.Name: Bugaboo
user_reviews.Description:
Breakfast buffet at The Square Restaurant – Novotel Danang is truly impressive, offering a wide selection from pastries and fresh fruits to Vietnamese dishes like banh mi, congee, and shrimp soup. The pastry section stands out with buttery croissants, lemon madeleines, and gluten-free banana bread. The Vietnamese corner is also lovely, where guests can make their own banh mi with pate, pickles, and sauces.
The space is bright and comfortable with a nice view of the Han River. Special thanks to Luong, one of the restaurant staff, for being so kind, attentive, and professional — making the whole experience even better.
A perfect place to start your morning in Danang!

user_reviews.Name: Đoàn Thị Như Nguyệt -AH22A
user_reviews.Description:
The buffet breakfast was excellent, with a wide selection of both Asian and Western dishes. From fresh pastries, fruits, and juices to hot items like noodles, eggs cooked to order, and local specialties – everything was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented. The coffee was rich and aromatic, a perfect way to start the day.

I also had the chance to enjoy the BBQ night, which was truly memorable. The buffet featured a great variety of grilled seafood, meats, and vegetables, all perfectly seasoned and cooked right on the spot. The outdoor setting created a cozy yet lively atmosphere, and the attentive staff ensured every guest was well looked after.

What truly made my stay at Novotel unforgettable was the outstanding service. I would especially like to thank Ms. Nhung, who was incredibly attentive, friendly, and professional. She always welcomed us with a warm smile and went above and beyond to make sure we felt comfortable and satisfied. Her thoughtful service made us feel genuinely cared for and added a personal touch to the whole experience.

user_reviews.Name: Totoro E. T.
user_reviews.Description:
Restaurant in hotel usually have good chef and good food even though the price is much higher than other outside restaurants .
I went to there Saturday night buffet.  Oh I could say there seafood are all previously frozen and did not defrost properly so that all the meat in the crab and the shrimp are all mushy and doesn't even taste like freshly frozen seafood.  Except the sashimis and sushi are acceptable in terms of texture and no foul smell of odor.
I pay extra to have half a lobster but the texture of lobster meat is also mushy and very little firmness of the meat.  It's another indication that the lobster was frozen and not properly defrost.
The beef or not very tender and lack of flavor.
There are few Vietnamese dishes that they were serving which tasted pretty good.
Their ice cream taste more like sherber ice cream which taste like ice not cream.
For the price I pay I think the food that they serve are not worth it to go back again.

user_reviews.Name: Invoker
user_reviews.Description:
Breakfast at The Square was truly a delightful experience. The buffet offered an impressive variety of both local and international selections, beautifully presented and constantly replenished. I especially enjoyed the tropical fruit corner – fresh rambutan, passion fruit, watermelon, and other seasonal delights, all perfectly chilled and full of flavor.
The beverage station was equally thoughtful, with different choices from freshly brewed coffee to tea and juices, along with options like condensed milk and syrups for those who enjoy customizing their drinks. Everything was arranged with care, making it very easy to enjoy a personalized breakfast.
The atmosphere itself felt warm yet refined – modern décor, elegant lighting, and attentive staff who were always available but never intrusive. It strikes that perfect balance of comfort and sophistication.
Overall, The Square at Novotel sets a wonderful tone for the day – a breakfast that feels both nourishing and memorable. Highly recommended for anyone looking to start their morning with quality, variety, and a touch of elegance. 🌿

user_reviews.Name: Joshua Suh
user_reviews.Description: I’m Korean-American, have lived in Korea for more than a year, and this is the most authentic, tasty Korean food I’ve tried outside of K-towns in the U.S. or Korea! I love cold noodles, and this place nailed it with the flavor and texture of noodles. Side dishes (banchan) was also tasty, the kimchi was fresh, and free refills! The price and portion sizes were definitely reasonable given its touristy location (120,000 dong for a bowl of cold noodles). Highly recommend!

We had an amazing dining experience at manvar restaurant in Vietnam. The food was absolutely delicious and tasted just like authentic Indian cuisine. Every dish was perfectly cooked, full of flavor, and reminded us of home.
The staff was very friendly, polite, and welcoming. The service was quick and professional, and they made sure we were comfortable throughout our visit. The ambiance of the restaurant was clean, cozy, and beautifully maintained.
It’s not easy to find truly authentic Indian taste outside India, but this place exceeded our expectations. Highly recommended for anyone craving real Indian food in Vietnam!
We will definitely visit again. Thank you for the wonderful experience!

user_reviews.Name: Rahul Chandrashekar
user_reviews.Description:
Our guide took us to this restaurant for lunch. Glad that he did so. This was a better Indian restaurant in the city as all the dishes ordered were tasty. Including the strawberry Lassi which tasted like a real Lassi. The Tandoori Roti was so soft and had a good texture. The subzi was good too. The Jamoon was alright. The service was a bit slow though.
The price however is a bit on the higher side just like other Indian restaurants in the city.
We visited the restaurant in the afternoon and it was very warm inside though there were 4 ACs running inside the restaurant.
user_reviews.text_original:
Our guide took us to this restaurant for lunch. Glad that he did so. This was a better Indian restaurant in the city as all the dishes ordered were tasty. Including the strawberry Lassi which tasted like a real Lassi. The Tandoori Roti was so soft and had a good texture. The subzi was good too. The Jamoon was alright. The service was a bit slow though.
The price however is a bit on the higher side just like other Indian restaurants in the city.
We visited the restaurant in the afternoon and it was very warm inside though there were 4 ACs running inside the restaurant.
user_reviews.reply_text_original:
Dear Mr Rahul Chandrashekar,

Thank you for visiting Manvar Rasoi and for sharing your thoughtful review!

We're delighted to hear that you enjoyed the flavors of our dishes — especially the strawberry lassi, soft tandoori roti, and subzi. It's great to know we could offer you a satisfying Indian meal during your visit.

Regarding the warmth inside the restaurant, you're absolutely right — the afternoon heat in Da Nang can be quite strong, and the large glass windows, while giving a bright and open feel, can make it warmer despite having multiple ACs running.

We’d like to share that we also have another dining room without glass walls and with strong air conditioning, which stays much cooler. If you have the chance to visit us again, we’d be happy to seat you there for a more comfortable experience.

Thank you as well for your notes on service and pricing — every bit of feedback helps us improve.

Looking forward to welcoming you again soon!

Best regards,
Manvar Rasoi

user_reviews.Name: Chandeerah Davis
user_reviews.Description:
If I could give this restaurant a 6 star review I WOULD. First things first we were blown away by the amount of various options for every kind of Indian food. Both of us have traveled in India extensively and we let him know that the best biryani we've ever had was in Hyderabad. So our standards are high. We are also both from New York and DC where we get great Indian food. This place exceeded our expectations.

We love Indian food. Went by here one day while visiting the neighborhood and saw folks coming and going. So we went back a few days later.

It was delish. The host/manager and wait staff were kind and attentive.

Then we started to order and wow. Every dish was flavorful, well seasoned, the restaurant was very welcoming, with nice decor.

We had two mains, a chicken butter tikka and a prawn masala, and a shrimp biryani! All were perfectly spiced to our stated preferences. My partner loves spicy and her biryani and chicken tikka had a kick but weren't JUST spicy they were SPICED and the sauce was amazing.

My prawn masala came with perfectly steamed rice. We also had a flaky garlicky naan. A crispy sweet and savory honey chicken app. And some perfect whiskey gingers. At the end of our meal the manager even brought us out a sweet and chewy yummy rice pudding! Hints of cinnamon and he had no way of knowing rice pudding is my favorite!

He made LUNCH an experience and we WILL be back!!

title: Da Nang Rooftop - Craft Beer
address: Tầng thượng, 1B Lê Duẩn, Hải Châu, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: C R
user_reviews.Description:
What a spot for a great rooftop view of the city. Tucked in and up on the 4th floor of a somewhat sketchy building, the place itself is in good shape, and was nice and clean.

Reasonable selection for drinks, pricing is as expected, above normal beers, but below cocktail pricing. The servers are right there if you need them and they were friendly.

I would be coming here quite often if I lived in the area.

user_reviews.Name: Nikita Smolin
user_reviews.Description:
A truely amazing place! We come here almost everyday for beer and chill. The staff is awesome, the music is not too loud and adds a nice background. The prices are lower than in many bars around and they have promotions too! A nice river view adds to a romantic vibe

Highly recommend to enjoy the evening here! They usually close between 2 and 2 30, although sometimes earlier (if there are no customers)

Please come here, the business needs your support and they are highly professional and absolutely deserve your attention!

I drink quite many beers (5-6), so the price per person is a bit inflated

user_reviews.Name: Mao
user_reviews.Description:
Nice rooftop view and good location across from the Marriott, but honestly the beer was very disappointing. I tried two different beers and neither tasted good or even properly cold. I normally would not leave a negative review just for taste, but paying around 350,000 VND for a few average beers with added taxes felt completely unreasonable for the quality.

The atmosphere and view are nice, but the drinks did not match the price at all. Hopefully they improve the beer quality and serving temperature because right now it feels overpriced for what you get.

user_reviews.Name: Laura Garza
user_reviews.Description: We saw this place when riding to our hotel and wanted to check it out. I really like craft beers, and IPAs in particular, so I was excited to check it out. First off, I love the rooftop location. It's not too big and not too small. A perfect size overlooking everything! Next, the menu had a good selection of beers, but admittedly, when we went to order some IPAs, they did seem to be out (which kind of sucked, but I get it since it's not the only thing they offered). Last, we ordered some food to try out and overall it was good. We got the chicken skewers and cheesy fries. The cheesy fries were different than what I'm used to, but that's just because of location and not because of anything they did wrong. I embraced it and enjoyed the flavoring. Overall, it was a nice experience and would recommend for a relaxed vibe with friends, date, etc.

Thank you so much, dear Laura, for your wonderful 5-star review and your kind words about our restaurant! 😍

We’re truly happy to hear that you loved our rooftop space, the relaxed vibe, and enjoyed the food and beer selection. It means a lot to us that you embraced the local twist on the cheesy fries and had a great overall experience. 💝

We would absolutely love to welcome you back so you can finally enjoy the IPAs you were looking forward to. 🥰

Hope to see you again soon for more craft beers, great views, and good vibes at Da Nang Rooftop - Craft Beer! ✨

title: Rasoi Indian

user_reviews.Name: Madhu Tiwari
user_reviews.Description:
I had such a lovely experience at Rasoi Indian Restaurant in Da Nang! 🌟

I ordered ginger tea and chaat, and both were absolutely delicious. The ginger tea was perfectly comforting, and the chaat had just the right balance of flavors—authentic and truly satisfying.

What made the experience even more special was the people. The staff were incredibly friendly, welcoming, and attentive, which made me feel right at home.

If you’re craving Indian food in Da Nang, this place is definitely worth visiting. Great food, great vibes, and wonderful hospitality. Highly recommended! ❤️

Sandeep was very sweet and helpful at ther restaurant

user_reviews.Name: David Cunningham
user_reviews.Description:
Great restaurant with delicious food and very friendly staff. The restaurant caters well for vegetarians and vegans, although it would be even better if the menu clearly distinguished between vegetarian and vegan dishes.

The portions were quite small for rice and bread for the price, so we expected a little more food. Definitely more European-style pricing. Overall though, a lovely place with tasty food and welcoming staff.

user_reviews.Name: Satyam Gupta
user_reviews.Description:
If there was an option to give 0 stars, I would definitely choose that instead of 1.

We visited this restaurant in Da Nang after craving Indian food and honestly it turned out to be one of the worst restaurant experiences. We ordered idli and cheese dosa, and both were horribly prepared. The dosa was not cooked properly, tasted raw from inside, and the idli quality was equally bad. Food had no proper taste, no authenticity, and felt carelessly made.

On top of that, the staff attitude was horrible. Instead of listening to the complaint respectfully, they behaved rudely and acted like customers were the problem. Waiting so long for badly cooked food and then getting this kind of response was extremely disappointing.

This place is charging money for food that doesn’t even meet basic standards. If you are looking for good Indian food in Da Nang, please avoid this restaurant completely. There are far better places nearby. This restaurant will only ruin your mood, waste your time, and leave you frustrated.

user_reviews.Name: Ravi Ladia
user_reviews.Description:
Rasoi Indian Pure Vegetarian Restaurant is, without question, the best Indian restaurant in Da Nang by a clear margin. From the moment you walk in, the experience is dialed up across all dimensions—food quality, hospitality, ambience, and attention to detail.

The food is outstanding and authentically Indian. The paneer tikka is perfectly marinated and grilled, delivering the right balance of spice and smokiness. The veg makhanwala is rich, creamy, and comforting without being heavy—exactly how it should be. The rotis and parathas are fresh, soft, and cooked to perfection, pairing seamlessly with the gravies. Even the rice deserves a mention—well-cooked, aromatic, and clearly not an afterthought.

Hospitality here is excellent. The staff are warm, attentive, and genuinely invested in ensuring a great dining experience, not just going through the motions. Service is prompt and professional.

The restaurant itself has an undeniable Indian vibe—tasteful décor, welcoming energy, and a clean, well-maintained setup. A special mention for the clean washrooms, which speaks volumes about overall standards and management discipline.

If you are craving authentic vegetarian Indian food in Da Nang, Rasoi is not just a good option—it is the benchmark. Highly recommended.

user_reviews.Name: Harita Kakkad
user_reviews.Description:
We went here twice, day 1 experience was really good & food was yum as well this was around 11:30 pm in night. Second day HORRIBLE! Service was super slow (we went here in peak rush hour 9:00 pm) but 1 dish took 45 mins and then too horrible taste- so oily! We got food poisoning immediately the next day.
My advice is not to go here in rush hour they degrade the quality so much!!
Definitely dont order aloo chat (the worst thing here), paneer tikka & naan were good on day 1, biryani was ok.

Staff is nice and friendly!

They accept gpay which is extremely helpful.

title: Bladi Da Nang
address: 80 Tô Hiến Thành, An Hải, Đà Nẵng 55000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Cheri The Glutton
user_reviews.Description:
A true gem of Da Nang. It’s been a while since my last visit, but this Mediterranean restaurant is absolutely world-class.

The vegetables are vibrant and perfectly balanced with every ingredient. The braised lamb is incredibly luxurious, and the Moroccan Briwats (crispy triangular pastries) are a must-try—they pair perfectly with wine. The beef tartare was a masterpiece that blew me away with every bite.

This soulful and adventurous chef is at tucked away on a quiet street in a lush, green corner, it’s the kind of place that makes you feel truly grateful for life as you sip wine in the afternoon. I can't wait to go back!

This is exactly the spirit we wish to cultivate: a sincere, warm, and unpretentious place where one can take the time to savor a moment in life.
Your message is a true source of motivation for the entire team. We hope to have the honor and pleasure of welcoming you back very soon.🫶🏽 🌿

user_reviews.Description: We received a very warm welcome from the chef and his team. We had already ordered Couscous Royal the day before. It was truly "Royal" and more than worth it; all the flavors were perfectly balanced. You can tell that Samir and his team cook with passion and dedication, ensuring that all his guests go home satisfied. Nadia and I left feeling very satisfied; it felt like dining with family. Samir, thank you so much for this wonderful experience. I highly recommend it to everyone; we were pleasantly surprised to have found a Moroccan restaurant of this high standard in Vietnam.

user_reviews.Description: The manager is very kind—from the warm welcome to the great attention to customers. My mom and I ordered a burger and a pizza. He then gave us a discount, which made us feel very touched and grateful. Thank you so much. If we come to Da Nang again, we will definitely come back.

user_reviews.Name: Rico Maksoud
user_reviews.Description:
If you’re in Da Nang and craving real Moroccan and Halal food, Beladi is an absolute gem. Samir the owner is incredibly warm and welcoming — the kind of hospitality that instantly makes you feel at home.

The harira is the best I’ve ever had, the tagine is packed with flavor, the briouats are perfect, and even the pizza is amazing.

It’s honestly one of the best food experiences I’ve had on this trip.

Highly recommended❤️

title: Doc moc buffet
address: 209/5 Nguyễn Văn Thoại, An Hải, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Roland Larson
user_reviews.Description:
Excellent buffet experience with a fantastic selection of fresh seafood. You can either grill it yourself on the barbecue stations or choose from the ready-to-eat options in the bain-maries.
The staff were incredibly friendly and attentive, particularly helpful for those unfamiliar with the self-cook process. When the chef noticed we'd never prepared fresh crab before, he kindly offered to cook them and crack them for us - that's the kind of service that makes a difference.
Finished off with ice cream and fresh fruit. Highly recommended for anyone looking for quality seafood and a fun, interactive dining experience.

user_reviews.Description: It's a pretty looking restaurant. I enjoyed my grilled scallion oil oysters and scallops. The ready made food was decent too. It wasn't mindblowing but a good place for dinner :)

user_reviews.Name: LiaoShin
user_reviews.Description:
An incredible seafood buffet experience in Da Nang!
• Seafood: The highlight is definitely the live seafood tanks right at the restaurant with a wide variety: flower crabs, giant river prawns, clams, and more. The pre-cooked dishes are also impressive, especially the grilled oysters with scallion oil, various snails, and fresh spring rolls.
• Ambiance: The restaurant features an open-space design with very tasteful decor. The use of ropes and wooden lanterns creates a cozy and rustic vibe that lives up to its name, 'Độc Mộc'. It’s spacious and perfect for both tourists and locals, whether it's for a family dinner or a birthday party.
• Service: The staff are quick and attentive. The food stations are constantly refilled, so you never have to wait long. I also loved the variety of dipping sauces – they have everything from classic lime-salt-pepper to signature spicy sauces.
• Location: Conveniently located on Nguyen Van Thoại street, right in the heart of the tourist area and very close to the beach.
Highly recommended and I will definitely be back!

Amazing seafoods alive and cooked plenty options you can choose yourself
a waiter (glassy cute man) helped me with all those grilled seafoods without me asking, very much appreciated ❤️
They are welcome a single person.
Price was 399 which acceptable for me.
Do not miss the hot pot at the end it was recommended even I was extremely full but still I love it ! 100% recommend it 🥰🥰👍🏼 almost forgot, if you come with children. Playground is available

user_reviews.Name: then yue wong
user_reviews.Description:
Don't make mistake like us. We didn't allocate enough of time for this dinner.

Its a buffet BBQ seafood dinner. It was quite peak for the restaurant. The place is packed. Its a bit under-staffed for that kind of crowds.  Charcoal was slow to be put onto our table. The cooking takes time. In order to really enjoy this, we would probably need at least 2 hours to dine here.

The vibe is great, it not hot surrounding since its considered winter time. Its quite clean, the dishes were presented nicely and arranged properly. The live and raw seafood are at another section.

Superbly worth the money paid for this buffet (if you like seafood)

If there is another opportunity, I would come back here to enjoy a proper dinner, take my sweet time to bbq and chit chat with friends and families.

title: NU ĐỒ
address: 11/1 Lưu Quang Thuận, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam
plus_code: 26RW+95 Ngu Hanh Son, Da Nang, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Stevie B
user_reviews.Description:
Today my wife and I meet up with some very dear friends as they highly recommended this quaint cafe.
We were not disappointed.
We all had the fish noodles,  spring rolls, tea and coconut water.
Ok, let's just start by saying everything was FANTASTIC!!
The noodles are cooked to perfection, the fish is tender and very flavorful and all herbs and salad were so fresh 😋
I had the honor and privilege. of meeting the owner, the chef and all the amazing staff.
Thank you all for a lovely experience and making my heart ❤️ smile.
The food is made with love   history and the pride of family.
I highly recommend this place; the food and the atmosphere makes you feel  very special.
We will be back very soon.

user_reviews.Name: Дмитрий Грамагин
user_reviews.Description:
I had a wonderful experience at NU ĐỖ.
The food is truly authentic Vietnamese cuisine — fresh, balanced, and full of flavor. Every dish felt thoughtfully prepared and beautifully presented.

What made the visit even more special was the atmosphere. The place is calm, cozy, and very welcoming. The chef and owner is absolutely charming and clearly cooks with care and passion, which you can really feel in the food.

Service was excellent — attentive, warm, and genuine without being intrusive.

A small place with a big heart. I’ll definitely come back and would happily recommend it to anyone looking for real Vietnamese food in Da Nang.

user_reviews.Name: Danny Chung
user_reviews.Description:
Our favorite meal in Vietnam! Go in the morning before the rush begins!!!

The noodles were perfectly cooked with the flavorful broth beneath them. We tried the chicken and beef noodles, which were equally incredible. The herbs balanced the umami and saltiness of the noodles and the spring rolls were light and packed with filling.

The chef also stopped by to check in on us and she was sooo kind. Will definitely come again before our trip is done.

user_reviews.Name: Billy C
user_reviews.Description: We both had the fish mi quang. One of the best things we have ever eaten. Delicious broth, perfectly cooked noodles and tasty, tumeric/curry fish. The spring rolls and iced milk coffees were amazing as well. The setting and service were wonderful! We will be back to try the beef and chicken.

The spring rolls and iced milk coffees were amazing as well. The setting and service were wonderful! We will be back to try the beef and chicken.

user_reviews.Name: TravelGuru Worldwide
user_reviews.Description:
This is our 3rd time here this visit. We have eaten all over Da Nang and there are plenty of amazing eateries in Da Nang but we were really struck by Nudo Kitchen for their artistry in blending traditional flavors with the modern diet. We especially love that their proteins do not come with bone, which is rare in local eateries here. We love the high quality and beautifully spiced fish, beef, chicken, and even their vegetables. Everything in the bowl of noodle is scrumptious looking and you don’t have to pick out bone and fat off the meat like other places. We absolutely love that.

This oasis eatery delivers on both flavor and atmosphere. The noodles are well executed and clearly made with care, with clean broth and balanced seasoning rather than relying on heaviness or excess oil. Portions are satisfying without feeling overwhelming. The space is comfortable and thoughtfully designed, which makes it an easy place to linger (and sip more on their amazing coffee). Service was generally attentive, though a bit slower when they were bombarded by large crowds.

We are off to Hanoi for NYE but already excited to return to Da Nang and eat at Nudo. We’ll likely just eat here for the last two days that we are in Da Nang before we head to Singapore.

user_reviews.Name: Teo Jia Chern (Justin)
user_reviews.Description: Great food, cool ambience, friendly staff, simple menu, each of the mi quang (chicken, fish, beef) comes with their own individual broth/flavour and it all blends well together. Highly recommend the milk coffee and spring rolls. They’ll give an apron to prevent spilling from accidental slurps. Had a chance to talk to the owner twice and she’s a lovely, friendly and cool person. Loved this place so much we had to come back twice!

user_reviews.Name: P B
user_reviews.Description:
This place serves some of the best noodle soup I’ve ever had — absolutely delicious and perfectly balanced in flavor. Whether you choose fish, chicken, beef, or the vegetarian option, every bowl is rich, fresh, and deeply satisfying.

At around 100k VND per bowl, it’s a bit pricier than regular street food, but totally worth it for the quality and taste you get. The atmosphere and presentation match the Michelin standard — a must-try spot in Da Nang!

user_reviews.Description: The experience and the service was just the best I’ve experienced in Vietnam. And a great price for a noodle dish you can plainly see they are very proud of. Highly recommend this place for an authentic experience. I tried the chicken noodles and loved them.

title: Cơm Nhà Linh
address: 35 An Thượng 26, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam
plus_code: 364V+4W Ngu Hanh Son, Da Nang, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Clo
user_reviews.Description: Lovely atmosphere, great bamboo panelled restaurant playing nice traditional music. Vietnamese food- really nice and fresh done well and served really beautifully in blue and white bowls and on wooden trays with leaves. We had bun cha and lemon grass pork (both were delicious and we have been travelling Vietnam for almost three months now)

user_reviews.Name: Khue Ho
user_reviews.Description:
I noticed this restaurant has several branches around Da Nang, so I decided to give it a try.

Overall, the food was decent — probably around a 6/10 for me. Nothing particularly bad, but nothing especially memorable either.

One thing I found a bit disappointing was that the kitchen seemed quite inflexible. We ordered bún chả, and at many places, it’s usually possible to order extra items separately — for example, an extra portion of meat or an additional spring roll for an extra charge. However, when we asked if we could pay for extra meat, we were told that we had to order an entirely separate full portion with noodles, herbs, and everything else, and the kitchen would not allow extra toppings on their own.

The restaurant itself was clean, and I liked that they had a self-service sauce station, which was convenient. The staff, however, seemed a little low-energy / not very enthusiastic.

Overall, it was an okay experience, but I do think a bit more flexibility and warmth in service would make a difference.

user_reviews.Name: irina IVA
user_reviews.Description:
Beautiful interior, but a total disappointment for foodies.

✅The Good:

🌴🌿Ambiance: Stunning design, lovely bamboo tables, and a very relaxing color palette. The place looks great!

🥢🌯Appetizers: The spring rolls were the only edible part of the meal, though quite oily.

❌The Bad:

🍽️Menu Balance: Mostly huge sets for groups; very few options for solo diners or couples. Also, no Pho Bo on the menu!

🥞The "Oil" Pancake: We've had Vietnamese pancakes about 20 times before, but this was the worst. It had no flavor other than pure grease-it was literally swimming in oil.

👎The Verdict:

After looking at the menu, we wanted to leave, we stayed only because we didn't want to be rude to the staff, but we should have listened to our gut and left. We gave it a chance, but now we're just left with a heavy stomach and regret. Beautiful place, but the food is a hard pass.

user_reviews.Name: Tarnoise Spaceman
user_reviews.Description:
Im viet, went alone intending to have a good  lunch here but got disappointed because the service was bad. First, the staff who did serving were quite unfriendly and impolite, food was put on table in unkindly manner, no introduction or notice.
I ordered boiled meet and a bowl of soup. The waiting for the meat is super long, soup and rice got cold alrd but meat has not come, took about 20mins. I asked to heat up the soup but took 15mins. Side dishes like pickles ran out but nobody tops it up.
Tho the price is considerably high, the portion for meat is surprisingly small, 11 pcs of thin belly pork for 140k, understand that they can charge that high but at least the keep service good.
Tho the food taste fine but my mood got killed miserably man.

user_reviews.Name: Ara
user_reviews.Description:
We ordered the stir-fried beef noodles and bún chả, and both dishes were absolutely delicious. The grilled pork in the bún chả was tender and flavorful, and the dipping broth was well-balanced. Portions were generous, making it a very satisfying lunch.

Everything was freshly prepared and served quickly. The staff were friendly, and the ambiance was clean and welcoming. There's air conditioning inside, which was perfect for the hot weather.

Highly recommended for a tasty and filling Vietnamese lunch!

user_reviews.Name: Valerie del Rosario
user_reviews.Description:
Hmm quite disappointing. We were there during TET and I get it - a lot of places are closed and some people need to work during the holidays but the service was terrible. The staff kept forgetting orders.. we had to ask for our coconut juice twice, they served us warm beer with no cups and no cutleries. And one of the staff was walking around with a wad of tissue stuck up her nose (you know twirled around as if to stop her nose from leaking..idk) so not professional. The place was not clean as well and the bins under the tables were overflowing, bits of food on the floor and I caught one of the staff simply grabbing tissue to clean a baby chair (this was after she used her hands to try to wipe the stuff off)

Now for the food itself - we ordered the fried spring rolls (it was ok), noodles with seafood (basically Lou min with seafood and veggies), red snapper with green mango strips (disappointed as it was just ok) and morning glory… for the price we paid (855k with taxes) - it was a bit meh.

user_reviews.Name: Russ Lacuata
user_reviews.Description: Authentic home style Vietnamese dishes. Everything is made fresh and prepared with love. Service is warm and attentive. The restaurant is clean. Love the wooden bamboo tables. The decor is simple but elegant. This is a great restaurant that everyone can enjoy. The prices are just right.
user_reviews.text_original: Authentic home style Vietnamese dishes. Everything is made fresh and prepared with love. Service is warm and attentive. The restaurant is clean. Love the wooden bamboo tables. The decor is simple but elegant. This is a great restaurant that everyone can enjoy. The prices are just right.

user_reviews.Name: DonkTSPirit
user_reviews.Description:
This rice restaurant truly gives you that comforting feeling of a home-cooked meal. From the moment you walk in, the space feels clean, tidy, and very welcoming. The dishes are served quickly while still being hot and fresh. The flavors are rich and well-balanced—not too greasy—making the meal satisfying without feeling heavy. What stands out most is the thoughtful seasoning; each dish has its own distinct taste rather than feeling repetitive.

The portions are generous, with a great variety of toppings, and you can tell the ingredients are carefully selected. The prices are very reasonable for the quality, definitely offering great value for money. The staff are also friendly, attentive, and quick, which makes the whole dining experience even more enjoyable.

Overall, this is a place I would gladly come back to many times and confidently recommend to friends and family. It’s an ideal spot for anyone looking for a delicious, cozy meal that truly captures the essence of Vietnamese home-style cooking.

user_reviews.Name: Joyce Rine
user_reviews.Description: I love the place. It is a place for a quiet and nice dinner. Their service is also good. I love the open kitchen style. The food is also good but one thing is it is quite hard to chew. We have expected the fried spring rolls to be crispy. The rice paper was not separated properly so it was all broken.

Dear Joyce Rine,

Thank you very much for your kind review and for sharing your experience with us. We’re delighted to know that you enjoyed the atmosphere, our open kitchen concept, and the service from our team.

At the same time, we sincerely apologize that the fried spring rolls did not meet your expectations. Your comments about the crispiness and the rice paper are very valuable, and we will share them with our kitchen team to improve the quality of the dish.

user_reviews.Name: Khue Ho
user_reviews.Description:
The restaurant is spacious and beautifully designed. The food is delicious, with a comforting homemade style.

The clay pot rice is tasty and nicely crispy without being hard, though it’s a bit oily inside compared to my preference for lighter flavors. Vietnamese Pomelo Sweet Gruel dessert is served warm, with a very mild sweetness—not overly sweet like many other places—absolutely delicious, 5 stars. The black sticky rice dessert is good but slightly sweet, 4 stars.

The service is friendly and polite. On our first visit, the restaurant was quite empty, but the braised pork with shrimp took a while to be served. On our second visit, I asked in advance how long certain dishes like clay pot rice would take—they said 15–20 minutes, and the food arrived exactly within that time.

We’ll definitely be coming back.

title: Voi's Kitchen 1
address: 108 Thủ Khoa Huân, An Hai, An Hải, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Shandi B
user_reviews.Description:
My family of four had breakfast at Voi's, and it was an outstanding experience from start to finish. We ordered the Nutella pancakes, scrambled eggs with tomato and onion served with a side salad, and the avocado toast topped with mushrooms and tomatoes, also with a side salad. We also tried the hash browns, bacon, and toast as sides, along with coffee and juice.

Everything was absolutely delicious. The food was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully prepared. Every dish was enjoyed by by family, and it was easily one of our favorite breakfasts during our trip.

What really made the experience special was the exceptional service. The staff checked on us throughout our meal to make sure everything was perfect and were genuinely warm and attentive. We accidentally broke a cup and offered to pay for it, but they kindly refused.

The combination of amazing food, excellent coffee, and truly outstanding hospitality made this a memorable visit. We highly recommend Vio to anyone looking for a fantastic breakfast and top-notch service. We will definitely be back! 😊

user_reviews.reply_text_original:
Hi Shandi B,

Thank you so much for your incredibly thoughtful review 😊

We're truly honored that your family chose to have breakfast with us and that it became one of your favorite meals during your trip.

It’s wonderful to hear that everyone enjoyed the Nutella Pancakes, scrambled eggs, avocado toast, and all the sides and drinks. Our team puts a lot of care into preparing each dish with fresh ingredients, so knowing that the food was flavorful, beautifully presented, and enjoyed by the whole family means so much to us.

We're especially grateful for your kind words about our team. Providing genuine hospitality is at the heart of what we do, and we're delighted that our staff helped make your visit memorable. Thank you as well for your understanding regarding the broken cup, these things happen, and we're simply glad you had a comfortable and enjoyable experience with us.

Your recommendation means the world to us. Reviews like yours are a wonderful encouragement to our entire team and remind us why we love what we do.

We can't wait to welcome you and your family back to Voi’s Kitchen for another delicious breakfast and warm hospitality on your next visit to Da Nang. 

Warm regards,
Voi’s Kitchen Team

user_reviews.Name: Josh Lin
user_reviews.Description:
We came here today because of the high rating and reviews and wanted to check it out. The food was genuinely tasty, and the overall environment feels warm, cozy, and nicely designed. Service was fine and the experience was pleasant.

The only downside for us is the price. It’s noticeably higher than most coffee and brunch places in Da Nang, and while the food is good, the value didn’t feel worth it for what we paid. Because of that, I’m not sure we would come back regularly, even though we enjoyed the meal.

If budget isn’t a concern and you want a cozy brunch spot, you’ll probably like it. If you’re value-focused, just be aware it’s on the more expensive side.

user_reviews.Name: Yadjiev
user_reviews.Description:
Vio’s Kitchen was honestly one of the best places I tried in Da Nang.

I went there for breakfast and really enjoyed it. The poached egg with toast was very good. The yogurt bowl with fruit was fresh and well balanced — banana, raisins, dragon fruit, mango, honey, plus pumpkin and chia seeds, which was a nice touch.

The coconut coffee with ice was excellent and probably my favorite. It came with coconut flakes on top, and the combination with the coffee flavor worked really well.

The service was great. At the beginning they bring wet towels to clean your hands and serve water with a slice of lemon. The staff were very friendly, smiling, and clearly attentive, which made the experience pleasant.

If I had to suggest small improvements: the toast could absorb the egg yolk a bit better, the chairs could be more comfortable, and the blender noise could be slightly reduced or placed farther from the seating area.

Overall, really good food, warm service, and a place I would happily come back to.
⋰Ẍ⋱
user_reviews.text_original:
Vio’s Kitchen was honestly one of the best places I tried in Da Nang.

I went there for breakfast and really enjoyed it. The poached egg with toast was very good. The yogurt bowl with fruit was fresh and well balanced — banana, raisins, dragon fruit, mango, honey, plus pumpkin and chia seeds, which was a nice touch.

The coconut coffee with ice was excellent and probably my favorite. It came with coconut flakes on top, and the combination with the coffee flavor worked really well.

The service was great. At the beginning they bring wet towels to clean your hands and serve water with a slice of lemon. The staff were very friendly, smiling, and clearly attentive, which made the experience pleasant.

If I had to suggest small improvements: the toast could absorb the egg yolk a bit better, the chairs could be more comfortable, and the blender noise could be slightly reduced or placed farther from the seating area.

Overall, really good food, warm service, and a place I would happily come back to.
⋰Ẍ⋱
user_reviews.reply_text_original:
Hi Yadjiev, 

Thank you for taking a time to review us. We are glad that you had excellent experience on your recent visit.

We are happy that you loved our café with great food and great service that you mentioned.
Especially the avocado toast with the poached eggs, fruits yoghurt that balanced, and the coconut coffee that become your favorite. 
Thank you for the honest feedback, we have noted and we appreciated that for improvement. 
Thank you for enjoying the moments and recommendation, we can’t wait to see you again ❤️

Thank you.
Voi's Kitchen Team

user_reviews.Name: Aidan & Marybeth
user_reviews.Description:
Really nice cafe, staff were very polite and friendly, good did not take long, and tasted great. Honestly, a nice little food spot, that was not really busy. Definitely worth a visit - we recommend the smoothie bowls 😄

Cafe was quiet enough to talk - and there was plenty of room both inside and outside seating. Overall, reasonably priced compared to some other cafes in the area - will return here when we come back to Danang in the future 🙂

user_reviews.Name: Janice Edwards
user_reviews.Description:
I’ll start by saying the service at Voi’s is unmatched in Da Nang. The staff is ON IT! They’re very nice and always there to help or clear something from your table.

I didn’t capture a photo of my partners breakfast of an egg sandwich, but it was a tasty breakfast sandwich. The fresh squeezed juices we very fresh and would’ve made delicious mimosas. The pancakes were a bit dense and not my favorite and I would not get them again. It is on the higher end of prices for breakfast, but we liked Voi’s better than other breakfast spots in the area.

user_reviews.Name: EVGENY IVANOV
user_reviews.Description:
I had a very positive experience at Vois Kitchen. The interior is well-designed and has a nice, modern feel to it—very clean and aesthetically pleasing.
As for the food, the presentation is excellent; everything looks carefully prepared. More importantly, the taste matches the visuals. The flavors are balanced, and the ingredients are clearly fresh and of high quality.
It’s a solid choice in Da Nang if you're looking for a beautiful environment and a consistently good meal. 5 stars.

user_reviews.Description: Our recent visit to City High Dining, where we celebrated a friend's birthday, was nothing short of exceptional. The staff's warm hospitality and the beautifully presented, delicious dishes crafted by talented chefs exceeded our expectations. user_reviews.Name: Shafiee Yakob
user_reviews.Description:
I have to say, I was pleasantly surprised by the location of this place! But once I stepped inside, the ambiance was just fantastic – both elegant and cool at the same time. I was warmly welcomed with a friendly "xin chao" and escorted to my table. I actually found this gem of a restaurant on Google and decided to book a table for myself, making sure to mention my dietary preferences of no nuts, pork, and wine.

Let me tell you, the food exceeded all my expectations! The Hokkaido scallop was perfectly cooked, the wagyu was absolutely delicious, and the dessert was so refreshing. I can't express how happy I am with my orders. I have to give this restaurant a solid 5 stars. Trust me, the prices were reasonable and totally worth it!

user_reviews.Description: We made a wrong turn to our restaurant of choice and ended up at Pause and Enjoy Danang. No regrets. We were warmly welcomed by a friendly server, Thor. user_reviews.Name: Lin Hanrong
user_reviews.Description:
The restaurant is super cozy as it is decorated with Christmas lights and tree. The interior radiates a warm lighting which is perfect for picture taking!

Food is well cooked and super delicious. The pho is very fragrant and the side dishes are hot and nicely seasoned. At the end of the meal, there is even a plate of fruit on the house!

Service is on spot as well. The staff can speak many languages. When I enter the restaurant, I am instantly greeted and felt welcomed.

Overall, it’s a must go not only for tourists, but for locals as well! Additionally, food is relatively cheap at $15+ for 2 bowls of noodles and 2 sides.

PHENOMENONAL service from Thor!

user_reviews.Name: Marián Fůsek
user_reviews.Description:
We’d passed this place so many times, always sensing its calm, peaceful vibe. Finally, after five months, we went — and what an amazing spot it is.
From the first moment, the staff was fantastic: positive, smiley, helpful — just perfect. We ordered Bánh Mì Chảo and Bánh xèo. I can only speak for my Bún chả... 10/10. After a few bites, I was already planning my next visit.
Go and enjoy. It’s a beautiful, minimal yet cozy, spotless place tucked on a quiet street.
Absolutely goes straight to my list of top spots in Da Nang.

user_reviews.Description:
My wife's favourite steak place so far.
We arrived without a reservation and were warmly welcomed by the staff, who still managed to accommodate us despite the restaurant being packed. The service was impeccable throughout the evening, with the team genuinely going above and beyond to ensure we had a great dining experience.
The food was excellent from start to finish, including the sides, and the steaks were cooked to perfection exactly as requested. Every dish was well executed, making for a thoroughly enjoyable meal.
The only minor issue was that the downstairs toilet was clogged and temporarily unusable. However, this appeared to be an unfortunate oversight rather than a recurring problem. The staff handled the situation professionally, apologised sincerely, and promptly directed us to the upstairs facilities.
Overall, a fantastic dining experience with outstanding food and service. We would definitely return and highly recommend it to fellow steak lovers. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

user_reviews.Name: Dave Ormes
user_reviews.Description: Butchers Shop steakhouse absolutely blew me away. I ordered the ribeye, and it was easily one of the best steaks I’ve ever had. It was perfectly cooked, incredibly tender, and packed with rich, flavor. Every bite was exceptional, and it’s clear they take real pride in the quality of their food. The 3 other people I dined with also agreed. If you’re a steak lover, this place is an absolute must. We will probably return here tomorrow evening!!!

user_reviews.Name: Nora
user_reviews.Description:
​I visited for a birthday celebration and had a very mixed experience. On the positive side, the filet mignon was great and cooked well. However, the ribeye was only okay, and the sides were a major letdown. The fries were very cold and hard, though the mushrooms were decent.
​The most concerning part of the night was seeing what looked like black mold growing on the ceiling directly above a table on the second floor. When we pointed this out and asked to move, the staff seemed well aware of the issue but appeared completely indifferent. It is disappointing to see such a lack of concern for hygiene and customer comfort, especially when visiting for a special occasion. I won't be returning.

user_reviews.reply_text_original:
Dear Ms. Nora,

Thank you very much for choosing our restaurant to celebrate your birthday and for taking the time to share your detailed feedback about your experience.

We sincerely apologize for the mold issue on the second-floor ceiling, which affected your experience. Cleanliness and safety are always our top priorities. We immediately asked our technical team to inspect and thoroughly fix the area. We are also reviewing this matter with our staff to ensure that all customer concerns are handled seriously and responsibly.

We are pleased to know that the filet mignon met your expectations. However, we are very sorry that the ribeye and side dishes, especially the French fries, did not meet our standards. This is not the level of quality we aim to provide. We have shared your feedback with our kitchen team to review our preparation and serving process to ensure all dishes are served in the best condition.

We truly regret that your special occasion was not perfect, and we hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back and serve you better in the future.

Sincerely,
Butcher Steak team.

user_reviews.Name: Lee Wieden
user_reviews.Description: My Hubby and I came to Butcher yesterday for an early lunch, I ordered the flat Iron Steak and my hubby got the American Breakfast. I really enjoyed my steak, it was cooked medium rare to my liking and a great gravy to pour over it, wasn't alot of vegetables on the plate, I guess I was expecting abit more than what I got, it was still enjoyable. Hubby was abit disappointed with the sausage he got in his American breakfast, it tasted abit off, he gave me a bite and I had to spit it out unfortunately, something wasn't right with the Sausage, the rest of the plate he enjoyed though. We have eaten here a few times over the years and was always good. Their Latte Coffee is really good, so much so I had to get 2, loved the brown sugar to add to the coffee ☕️, also the 2 fresh hot breadrolls you get with your meal. We often buy fresh meat on the Butcher side as well to take home with us, thats always handy. Parking can be quite an issue, if your walking not a problem, but parking a bike is a pain. Anyway we will keep coming back.

user_reviews.Name: Caloy Malonzo
user_reviews.Description: We spent our last night in Da Nang at Butcher Steak, and it was such a great choice. I ordered the sirloin steak cooked medium, and it came out just right—tender and flavorful. The place was clean and cozy, and the staff were warm and friendly. There’s even a small grocery next door, which I think is where they get their fresh meat and produce. Overall, it was the perfect way to wrap up our adventures in Vietnam.

user_reviews.Name: AW
user_reviews.Description: We had a great dinner at Butcher Steak, conveniently located near Haian Hotel & Spa. The steaks were well cooked, juicy, and flavourful, and the portions were generous. Service was excellent — attentive and friendly, which made the experience even better. A reliable and enjoyable steak option in Da Nang, especially if you’re staying nearby.

user_reviews.Name: Ciara Nicole
user_reviews.Description: We had our first proper meal here after a whole day of touring Bana Hills and it's one of the best meals we've had here in Da Nang. The steaks were a perfect medium rare, the salads and sides were fresh but not overly salty. The servers are also very attentive and check up on you which adds to the ambiance of the place, warm people and great service. It's a meal we'll remember from this trip. 😊

title: Horizon Restaurant & Bar
address: 120 Võ Nguyên Giáp, Street, An Hải, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Dawn
user_reviews.Description: Thank you to the staff at Horizon Bar for making our stay at Four-Point Sheraton so comfortable with their exceptional service standards. Special mentions to Trinh and her colleague for making us feel so at home for 2 beautiful evenings dining and drinking at Horizon Bar. The view was amazing and service standards were beyond expectations. The beers served were top notch and truly reflected the beauty and complexities of Da Nang.

I was not an in-house guest. I only came for dessert and cocktails.
The quality was quite good.
All staff were friendly and polite.
Ms. Trinh and Mr. Quan were very kind and helpful.
Thank you for the nice service.

user_reviews.Name: Adam Tward
user_reviews.Description: What an amazing experience! The food was top notch, the view was absolutely incredible, and the service was above average. We were lucky enough to be there when they had a live singer. I usually don’t like live singing but this guy was incredible! Great voice and seamless transitions between songs. It really added to the experience. Our server was so sweet and helpful, gave us excellent food recommendations and then took the time to show us some local places to get coffee and other local delicacies. I can’t sing the praises of this place enough. Would give 10 stars if possible.

user_reviews.Name: Sally Lim
user_reviews.Description: Went for the fireworks dinner buffet. Service was bad. Dirty plates were not clear promptly.  Water not refilled. Prawns not fresh. Ladle spoons for dessert all submerged inside bowls of desserts. Ice cream all melted. Staffs not able to check that the frozen fridge was not properly closed.  When fireworks started, restaurant lights were not fully off. We still can see alot humans reflections  on the window panels walking around and  also lift lobby lights . Super disturbing.  The high price for the buffet package was not worth it.

user_reviews.Name: Do Cuong
user_reviews.Description:
If you want a fancy but chill rooftop bar in Da Nang, you should totally check out Horizon Bar! It’s on top of the Four Points by Sheraton and has one of the best ocean and bay views.

The vibe here is really calm, even on weekends. You can enjoy some great cocktails, and the prices are pretty fair for a hotel bar. It's a perfect place to hang out, catch the sunset, and you don't have to deal with huge crowds.

title: Tom's Bar & Grill
address: 83C Ngô Thì Sĩ, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 50507, Vietnam
plus_code: 26XW+53 Ngu Hanh Son, Da Nang, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Steve Hilton
user_reviews.Description:
Service:
- Not overly welcoming or friendly, but since I was the only customer, I received my beer within a couple of minutes.
- Observation: One lady sat outside, but since nobody approached her as they were playing pool, she got up and left.
- Rating: 3/5

Atmosphere:
- During my visit there was no music, and although they had some TV’s they weren’t switched on.
- They also have a pool table, which I believe is free to play.
- To keep you cool, they have air conditioning, wall-mounted and portable fans.
- There’s a stage for a live band.
- Seating: All the seats were padded and comfortable
- The new decor is interesting.
- Rating: 2/5 (Hopefully it’s better in the evening)

My Order:
- 1 x Huda
- Served ice cold
- Price: 40K
- The price for Huda is definitely on the higher side.

Overall Rating: 3/5

Additional Information:
- The toilet was clean and had tissue paper.

user_reviews.Name: Sandy Werner
user_reviews.Description:
Toms Bar only closes at 3am and the kitchen is open late, which is amazing as the food here in general is exceptional.
FINAL EDIT: My last evening I tried the steak, against my better judgement as the steaks I'd had in Vietnam were terrible. This one, however changed the bar completely. It was delicious, well cooked for medium rare and by far the best steak I had in Da Nang. I'll let the photo tell the rest..
EDITED AGAIN: Last night I had the pork ribs. WOW! These are really amazing. So tasty and beautifully cooked. d210k (£5.88) if I remember correctly. Had a bottle of the house red, Argentinian Malbak which was also very nice at d585 (£16.38). Yes, wines in Da Nang are relatively pricey but all are imported.
ORIGINAL POST: Had the Creamy Chicken and Mushroom skillet and although there was a wait, it was well worth it. Served with mashed potatoes, It was deeeelicious!! Far better than I expected. d195k (£5.46). Service in general here is very good and beer prices also very reasonable with a Saigon 450ml coming in at just d40k (£1.12).
EDITED: Been back many times now. The ribs are exceptional. The fish is really good but don’t expect English style batter and fish. Only the burger and the fries I thought were average.

user_reviews.Name: Christine Lyse
user_reviews.Description:
I ordered the Crispy Chicken (the one clearly marked on the menu), but was served a completely different burger. On top of that, the burger was loaded with dressing - on top, in the middle, and on the bottom.
This was especially disappointing because I do not tolerate heavy dressing, and the only extra l requested was cheese.
When dining out, you expect to receive what you actually ordered, and for special requests to be respected. Instead, I ended up with a burger that was barely edible for me.
This shows poor attention to orders and lack of quality control, which seriously affected the overall experience. I expect much better handling of customer orders in the future.

user_reviews.Name: Thành An Võ
user_reviews.Description: Nice place,  yummy food, staff very friendly. Would highly recommended if you come to Da Nang.   🫰🏻🫰🏻🫰🏻
user_reviews.text_original: Nice place,  yummy food, staff very friendly. Would highly recommended if you come to Da Nang.   🫰🏻🫰🏻🫰🏻

user_reviews.Name: Travis Peacock
user_reviews.Description:
For the first time in my life I left a meal mostly uneaten and went to another restaurant. Usually I can at least get full enough on fries or something.

1) The food was awful: My fish smelled gross and was mushy. The batter on it was too salty (and my normal complaint is that nothing is salted enough). The chips were dry and flavorless. They had no salt or seasoning and salt wouldn't stick to them. The insides of the fries were like powdered mashed potatoes. I'm guessing either they didn't soak their fries properly or they are low quality frozen fries that have been in the freezer too long.

My partner's baked fish was also inedible. She was able to eat the salad at least.

2) My main waitress was nice enough, I asked for a bottle of water for my partner and I and then also a second bottle of water with a bowl for my dog. She didn't understand so she brought who I think was the manager or and I explained it to her and she said okay but then we got our water and there was no extra thing for my dog to drink so I ask for another cup which she brings me. We are sitting there talking and the woman who I think is the manager comes out, takes the cup and says the dog isn't allowed to drink out of it. Fine, then just give me something to let my dog drink from. She says no, nothing for dogs to drink out of... So I tell her we are going to leave then because I paid for water for my dog and explicitly said what it was for. When we start to pack up she comes out with a paper cup. Interesting so she DID have something, she just didn't want to give it to us until we threatened to leave.

If dogs aren't welcome that's fine, but the staff were friendly to my dog. Our waitress was petting it. So it didn't seem like she wasn't allowed.

Maybe she's just sad that she has to work at such a trash restaurant.

Photos are how much I started with and how much I left.

user_reviews.reply_text_original:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. we're genuinely sorry to hear that your recent experience with our food didn’t meet your expectations. We strive to provide the best quality and service, and it’s clear we fell short in this instance. 

We genuinely value your input and are committed to improving our offerings. 

As an apology and to show our appreciation for your feedback, we would like to invite you to join us for a complimentary meal. We hope this will give us the opportunity to provide you with the quality experience you expected. 

Please contact us directly at our Fb or IG to arrange a convenient time for your visit. We look forward to the chance to serve you better and restore your confidence in our restaurant. 

Sincerely,
Tom 
Owner

user_reviews.Name: Nguyễn Chí Bảo Official
user_reviews.Description: A really worthwhile place to visit — the staff are very friendly, the prices are quite reasonable, the music is great, and there are often dance groups hanging out there, creating a very emotional and vibrant atmosphere.
user_reviews.text_original: A really worthwhile place to visit — the staff are very friendly, the prices are quite reasonable, the music is great, and there are often dance groups hanging out there, creating a very emotional and vibrant atmosphere.

user_reviews.Name: All’sWell
user_reviews.Description:
Just had the hand baked pepperoni, 40 cm pizza at Tom’s
The best pizza I had in VN.
Crispy crust, great cheese, perfect pepperoni.
In Canada, we eat the pizza part first and save the crust till the end because the crust is never that good.
Here I found myself tearing off the crust and eating it first by itself because it was crispy tasty and perfect.
Eventually, I got to the Pizza.Part.
Awesome.
Best pizza crust in Vietnam, and I told the manager Quang that.
I ate my fill and gave the rest away to the waitresses and the patrons and everyone was totally happy which fit in nice with the music and vibe of this great bar.
Tom’s bar, in My An beach, Full menu, ice, cold, beers, good background, music, most comfortable chairs, and benches in Danang.
Check it out  !

title: BHOJAN INDIAN RESTAURANT
address: 20-22 Lê Quang Đạo, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 50000, Vietnam
plus_code: 363V+6R Ngu Hanh Son, Da Nang, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Mehul Rathi
user_reviews.Description:
Had a great dining experience at Bhojan Indian Restaurant. The rotis and breads were fresh and well-made, and the curries were full of flavor. The dal was comforting and authentic, and the Paneer Lababdar was definitely one of the highlights of the meal—rich, creamy, and delicious.

The biryani was decent, but I feel it could be improved a little in terms of flavor and aroma. That said, it didn't take away from the overall experience.

The restaurant has a pleasant ambience with good music playing in the background, creating a relaxing dining atmosphere. The staff were friendly, attentive, and welcoming throughout our visit.

Overall, a good Indian restaurant in Da Nang with tasty food, great service, and a nice vibe. Would happily visit again. 😊🍛🇮🇳

Overall, a good Indian restaurant in Da Nang with tasty food, great service, and a nice vibe. Would happily visit again. 😊🍛🇮🇳

user_reviews.Name: Utkersh Bora ubmuse
user_reviews.Description:
A very satisfying find in Da Nang, especially if you’re craving proper Indian food. Bhojan delivers familiar flavors that actually taste like home, not toned-down tourist versions.

The food was fresh, well-spiced, and comforting. The dal, paneer dishes, and breads were particularly good. Portion sizes are decent, and everything felt freshly prepared.

The staff were polite and welcoming, and service was reasonably quick even when the place was busy. The ambience is simple and clean—not fancy, but comfortable.

If I had to point out one small thing, it would be that the restaurant can get a bit crowded during peak hours, so service may slow down slightly. Also, the spice level is authentic, so it’s best to mention your preference if you want it mild.
