user_reviews.Name: Suman
user_reviews.Description:
I had a very good experience at Bhojan Indian Restaurant in Da Nang. The restaurant offers tasty Indian food with good quality and reasonable pricing. The overall ambiance is pleasant, and the place is clean and comfortable.
The staff were friendly, polite, and attentive, which made the experience even better. Our food was served within 15–20 minutes, which is quite reasonable and shows efficient service.
We ordered Veg Biryani, Noodles, and Manchurian, and all the dishes were fresh, flavorful, and well-prepared. The veg biryani had a nice aroma with balanced spices, the noodles were cooked perfectly, and the Manchurian was delicious with great taste and texture. Everything tasted authentic and satisfying.
Another great point is that the prices are affordable, especially considering the portion size and quality of the food. It’s a good option for anyone looking for tasty Indian food in Da Nang without spending too much.
Overall, Bhojan Indian Restaurant is a great choice for vegetarians and Indian food lovers. Good food, good service, reasonable waiting time, and affordable prices make it worth visiting. I would definitely recommend this restaurant and would love to visit again.

user_reviews.Name: Sanat Prabhudessai
user_reviews.Description:
I visited Vietnam this month, and after trying a lot of local food that didn’t quite match my taste, discovering Bhojan in Da Nang felt like a lifesaver! This place serves some of the best and most authentic Indian food I’ve had outside India.
The flavours were just perfect, the quality was top-notch, and every dish tasted just like home. It’s hard to believe one brand can maintain such high standards across six outlets in Da Nang, but Bhojan truly delivers. The food was fresh, flavorful, and absolutely satisfying.
The staff is warm, polite, and attentive, and the ambience makes you feel very comfortable. Whether you’re missing Indian food or just want a delicious and fulfilling meal, Bhojan is the place to go.
Also the owner is Indian and is very much friendly as well, you can also use pay in case you are low in cash or card
Highly recommended for anyone visiting Da Nang. This restaurant just made my trip so much better!

user_reviews.Name: Aniket Desai
user_reviews.Description: Friendly staff and great Indian food in Da Nang. All the dishes we ordered had an authentic taste and ambience was good as well. Chicken Lollipop and Onion pakoda were my favourites. Highly recommended if you’re after delicious food in the area.
user_reviews.text_original: Friendly staff and great Indian food in Da Nang. All the dishes we ordered had an authentic taste and ambience was good as well. Chicken Lollipop and Onion pakoda were my favourites. Highly recommended if you’re after delicious food in the area.

user_reviews.Name: M H
user_reviews.Description: There's more than 50 Indian restaurants in Da Nang, so it's difficult to figure out which ones are the best. Bhojan is definitely up there in the top 5 for me. The Hara Bhara veg kebab was a nice starter. Amazing garlic butter naan bread, stretchy and layered with generous seasoning. The best part was the Kadhai Paneer curry. Very rich flavors, interesting spices, great texture, delicious paneer (cottage cheese) and not too heavy. Staff are professional and friendly, and the restaurant is nicely decorated.

title: Adobo Mexican Grill
address: 23 An Thượng 2, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam
plus_code: 26XW+PW Ngu Hanh Son, Da Nang, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Jeff Sttricklin
user_reviews.Description:
Adobo Mexican Restaurant – A Fantastic Dining Experience

During our time exploring Da Nang, we had the pleasure of dining at Adobo Mexican Restaurant, and it certainly did not disappoint.

From the moment we walked through the door, the welcoming atmosphere set the tone for a great evening. The restaurant has a vibrant and relaxed feel, making it the perfect place to unwind after a day of sightseeing. The décor, music, and overall ambiance create an authentic and enjoyable dining experience.

The food was outstanding. Every dish was packed with flavour, prepared fresh, and beautifully presented. Whether you’re craving classic Mexican favourites or looking to try something new, Adobo delivers quality and taste in every bite. The generous portions and authentic flavours make it clear that a lot of care goes into every meal.

What truly stood out was the staff. The team was friendly, attentive, and genuinely passionate about providing excellent service. They were happy to answer questions, offer recommendations, and ensured that every guest felt welcome throughout the meal.

If you’re looking for great Mexican food, excellent service, and a fantastic atmosphere while in Da Nang, Adobo Mexican Restaurant is definitely worth a visit. A memorable dining experience that we would happily return to again.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Highly Recommended

A great combination of delicious food, friendly staff, and a lively atmosphere makes Adobo a standout dining destination.
Chiefs Wanderlust Adventures

user_reviews.Name: CJ
user_reviews.Description:
Adobo is a fast-casual Tex-Mex restaurant on An Thuong 2, and I dined in tonight (21/02/26).

I ordered the pulled beef burrito bowl on white rice (215k VND), with black beans, all the available toppings, three salsas, and two sauces — all included at no extra charge.

Wow. It was amazing. Everything tasted incredibly fresh and packed with flavor, and the portion size was huge. It was super satisfying and great value for what you get.

I also loved the hot sauce available on the table — the perfect extra kick.

The staff were friendly and helpful throughout.

I’ll definitely be returning.

user_reviews.Name: Cillian Ansems
user_reviews.Description: Been here four times now (twice through Grab, and twice in-eat). Every single time has been absolutely amazing. The portion sizes are so generous. Honestly, whoever set it as this is a maniac! (in the best possible way). It seems kinda expensive till the food comes out and it all makes sense. Highly recommend the pulled beef. I've had it on the buritto bowl and inside the buritto. Both outstanding. Quesadilla are also delicious. The staff are also super friendly. On our first visit we had Ryan look after us. And on our second visit in, he happened to be at the door and opened it us welcoming us back.

user_reviews.Name: Kimberly Lekey
user_reviews.Description:
Small place but seating on inside and outside. Friendly staff and food was delicious and hot. Only problem we had was the menu stated they take credit cards but when we were paying they only took cash. We had enough but it caught us by surprise. They said by next month they would be able to take credit.
Still a great place to eat and the food is worth coming back to!

user_reviews.Name: vinh lam
user_reviews.Description: Delicious tacos and burritos. Massive portion size and very fair pricing. This place reminds me of chipotle but wayyyy better. You don’t have to wait in a line to order but rather a server will come to your table and take your order. Service staff were quick and the food came out fresh and fast. Nice flavors for margaritas and bonus to open kitchen. It was nice to see the food being made fresh by the kitchen in the front 👍

user_reviews.Name: Oleg Valyn
user_reviews.Description:
This is the first Mexican place I’ve tried in Da Nang. Overall, it’s tasty, but expensive by Vietnamese standards. By international standards — reasonable.

The place itself feels more like a fast-food spot. That’s where there’s a mismatch: simple format, but above-average pricing. It’s clearly in a tourist area — mostly tourists eat here, locals wouldn’t pay these prices.

Dishes are around $7–8, while nearby places offer meals for $2–4 with decent portions.

We tried tacos — ordered chicken but got beef. Also had a burrito: very tasty, but the portion is quite large. For me, it was actually too much.

Overall — 4 out of 5. There’s room for improvement, especially in terms of atmosphere. For a Mexican place in a tourist area, one Frida Kahlo on the wall isn’t enough, especially at this price level.

user_reviews.Name: Craig Harris
user_reviews.Description:
Nice clean Mexican style restaurant. I had a pork burrito, which is my favorite type of Mexican food. The ingredients were really nice, though was a little let down by 3 things.
The menu indicated on google maps is only 9 months old & the cost at the restaurant has burritos at 35% more expensive. The food wasn't hot & they don't crisp up the tortilla. So the outside was very doughy, not light..
I would eat there again, but I would try something different. So close on the burritos, hope they get a hot plate to heat up the tortilla & food. Beer was nice..
Went back for lunch, their build your own salad was very nice, check their fresh juices as well.

title: Muối Biển Seafood Restaurant - Nhà hàng hải sản Đà Nẵng

user_reviews.Name: Isaac Sam
user_reviews.Description:
The food was genuinely delicious, fresh seafood, flavourful dishes, and beautifully presented plates that reflected the kitchen’s skill. The lobster, oysters, sashimi and other local favourites were especially memorable.

However, the dining tables were rather small and closely arranged, which made it difficult to fit all the dishes comfortably. It did feel a little cramped at times, especially with a larger spread.

Overall, the quality of the food made the visit worthwhile, and with some improvements to table spacing, the experience would be even better.

user_reviews.Name: Bryanna g
user_reviews.Description: Ordered 600g lobster and a coconut. The service was excellent. I had read it can be dry so I got the cheese thinking it would fill me up and give the dish some moisture. My fault I should have just had wine or butter grilled. The cheese was a sauce. It was just too much. I didn't mind the flavor though. The lobster was tender and sweet. I enjoyed the wasabi like vinegar that came for dipping so I would prefer to order something different next time. The reason for the low rating is that the bread served with the lobster was stale. For 1.5 million... Not worth it. I would like to try to again but order the wine poached or garlic next time but at the price perhaps I will try somewhere else for lobster and instead try some different dishes at this establishment. Service was great.

user_reviews.Name: Juan Martinez
user_reviews.Description: Beautiful beach, outdoor seating, service was good but a bit awkward in sequence and the food was tasty but not a good value. Was recommended by reviews and AI. Upon arrival we were immediately taken to the fish tanks that were clean and appropriately labeled. The staff was very helpful and courteous, we understood the concept having a similar experience in Hong Kong fisherman village. We selected scallops and lobster. Scallops were AMAZING, and the 0.9 kg lobster perfectly steamed in wine sauce, but I needed something for extra moisture, as there was no wine sauce. We asked for clarified butter which was brought after a few minutes. Unfortunately during that time the food was getting cold, but the temperature of the butter sauce compensated nicely. Staff was awesome, splitting and detaching the meat from shell. What was not good was the coordination between cooking seafood and getting everything else ordered. We sat at table outside with amazing view. We ordered a side seaweed salad with tuna that had lettuce, no seaweed and tuna from a can. This was so disappointing since Da Nang is known for tuna fishing, but I guess everything gets exported. Manager was diligent and honest and removed it from our bill. For that I commend management. The service timing was a bit off with the beautiful scallops showing up quite quickly before the “seaweed salad with tuna” and the side french fries ordered. The fries were hot and perfectly crispy. I strongly suggest removing that salad from your menu, as it is not at the same level of the rest of the food. Overall it was a good experience but then as we exit and went to restroom, wife was appalled by dead roach. I have friends that own restaurants and two elements that are indicative of a good establishment is the cleanliness of kitchen and restrooms. This might have been an exceptional circumstance but was the experience I lived, so please take feedback as constructive criticism and an opportunity for improvement. The food quality was good but relatively expensive for Vietnam, then again, Da Nang is very touristy so prices are high. I think there are better values in the area, but that is where I landed and made the best of my experience. Another negative was that the beer I got was barely above ambient temperature, and in the Vietnam heat, a cold beer is always best.
user_reviews.text_original: Beautiful beach, outdoor seating, service was good but a bit awkward in sequence and the food was tasty but not a good value. Was recommended by reviews and AI. Upon arrival we were immediately taken to the fish tanks that were clean and appropriately labeled. The staff was very helpful and courteous, we understood the concept having a similar experience in Hong Kong fisherman village. We selected scallops and lobster. Scallops were AMAZING, and the 0.9 kg lobster perfectly steamed in wine sauce, but I needed something for extra moisture, as there was no wine sauce. We asked for clarified butter which was brought after a few minutes. Unfortunately during that time the food was getting cold, but the temperature of the butter sauce compensated nicely. Staff was awesome, splitting and detaching the meat from shell. What was not good was the coordination between cooking seafood and getting everything else ordered. We sat at table outside with amazing view. We ordered a side seaweed salad with tuna that had lettuce, no seaweed and tuna from a can. This was so disappointing since Da Nang is known for tuna fishing, but I guess everything gets exported. Manager was diligent and honest and removed it from our bill. For that I commend management. The service timing was a bit off with the beautiful scallops showing up quite quickly before the “seaweed salad with tuna” and the side french fries ordered. The fries were hot and perfectly crispy. I strongly suggest removing that salad from your menu, as it is not at the same level of the rest of the food. Overall it was a good experience but then as we exit and went to restroom, wife was appalled by dead roach. I have friends that own restaurants and two elements that are indicative of a good establishment is the cleanliness of kitchen and restrooms. This might have been an exceptional circumstance but was the experience I lived, so please take feedback as constructive criticism and an opportunity for improvement. The food quality was good but relatively expensive for Vietnam, then again, Da Nang is very touristy so prices are high. I think there are better values in the area, but that is where I landed and made the best of my experience. Another negative was that the beer I got was barely above ambient temperature, and in the Vietnam heat, a cold beer is always best.
user_reviews.reply_text_original:
Thank you for choosing Muối Biển Seafood Restaurant. We strive to provide the highest quality cuisine and best service in Da Nang.

We sincerely appreciate your valuable comments and evaluations, and we look forward to continuing to provide you with delicious meals in the future. ♥️

thank you

Muối Biển Seafood Restaurant Team

user_reviews.Description:
Absolutely amazing seafood place! 🐟🦐

user_reviews.Name: Juliette Whittingslow
user_reviews.Description: You need to come here. I could just say this and hopefully my mission would be accomplished. But this vegan buffet deserves better than that. This food is DELICIOUS. Every bite I took of the many options it offered was incredible. I would dare say, ending my month of travels in Vietnam and eating in this restaurant was a treasure, best food ive had. I could be totally biased since it is my last day in Vietnam and im feeling emotional… but this place felt so healthy without sacrificing any of the flavorful. I am not kidding when I say this was one of the best places ive gone to. The amount of options are perfect. They offer simple black rice, or a spiced up to the perfect level yellow rice, soups, a couple of options with tofu, mushrooms, eggplant, many options with veggies, one or two salads. The mushrooms were so tasty as well as the eggplant. They even have a dessert made with the black beans I call adzuki which was very yummy. Their hibiscus tea is delightful, a tad sweet and sour, perfect combo. I could not recommend this place more. As soon as you walk in, you are welcomed by plants and a well thought out layout of the space. If you are looking for a delicious and healthy buffet, this is your go to.

title: Chops Da Nang
address: Lot 50 Trần Bạch Đằng, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Asyari Sharbini
user_reviews.Description:
A lovely Western-style burger joint with a talented singer performing a one-man acoustic set, creating a great atmosphere for a relaxed evening out.
Service was excellent, with friendly and attentive staff who made us feel welcome throughout our visit. The burgers were delicious and clearly the highlight of the meal. My wife’s chilli burger was the star of the show — packed with flavour and perfectly executed.
The hand-cut fries were a fantastic addition and complemented the burgers well. There was also a good selection of craft beers to enjoy alongside the food.
The only dish that didn't quite hit the mark for me was the fried breaded cheese, which I found a little overwhelming, though that ultimately comes down to personal preference.
Overall, a great spot for quality burgers, good drinks, and live music. We'd happily return for another visit. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

user_reviews.Name: Traveling Squid
user_reviews.Description:
Stopped by Chops to try their breakfast options as I heard great things about their burgers. I opted to have the Fluffy Pancakes, two scrambled eggs on the side, and a Carrot Topper drink. Food was good, no complaints. I wanted to try a few other breakfast options that looked enticing but was dining solo, so no opportunities to share.

My server, Elly, was fantastic. She greeted me with a smile, took my order and read it back for accuracy, and checked in during the meal to see if I wanted anything else, and whether the food was to my liking. As the morning sun started to settle on my table, she took steps to provide shade by adjusting the retractable awning.

Friendly staff , good food, and worth a visit. They have a variety of food options for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Indoor and outdoor dining options.

My breakfast meal was approximately
250,000 VND/$9.50 USD.
user_reviews.text_original:
Stopped by Chops to try their breakfast options as I heard great things about their burgers. I opted to have the Fluffy Pancakes, two scrambled eggs on the side, and a Carrot Topper drink. Food was good, no complaints. I wanted to try a few other breakfast options that looked enticing but was dining solo, so no opportunities to share.

My server, Elly, was fantastic. She greeted me with a smile, took my order and read it back for accuracy, and checked in during the meal to see if I wanted anything else, and whether the food was to my liking. As the morning sun started to settle on my table, she took steps to provide shade by adjusting the retractable awning.

Friendly staff , good food, and worth a visit. They have a variety of food options for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Indoor and outdoor dining options.

My breakfast meal was approximately
250,000 VND/$9.50 USD.
user_reviews.reply_text_original:
Thank you so much for taking the time to share such a detailed review! 😊 We’re really happy you enjoyed your breakfast and that the Fluffy Pancakes, eggs, and Carrot Topper hit the spot. It’s also great to hear you had a comfortable experience with our service and seating.

We’ll make sure to pass your kind words to Elly—she’ll be very happy to know her attentiveness made your morning better, especially adjusting the shade for you ☀️

We hope next time you visit, you’ll get to try even more of our breakfast options. Looking forward to welcoming you back again soon!

user_reviews.Name: Cheri The Glutton
user_reviews.Description:
This is the spot when you're craving something junk!
The American diner-style decor is fun and pop, and the staff are casual, friendly, and charming.

The "Sizzling Burger"—where they place a halved burger on a hot griddle and pour cheese sauce right over it—is such a visual treat. While the taste is exactly what you’d expect, it makes for a fun and memorable travel moment.

If you’re tired of Vietnamese food and want something heavy and satisfying, this is it!

user_reviews.Name: RaynSue “Northern Vagabonds”
user_reviews.Description:
We were really excited to go to Chops for Christmas Eve to have their turkey Christmas burger we sampled at the Christmas market a couple of weeks ago.  But unfortunately they don’t serve it in the restaurant.  Too bad. I think this was a failed marketing strategy.
We stayed anyway and our meal was good.
The server was great, he explained the menu and the types and sizes of the burgers and the staff were great the whole time. Definitely 5 stars for service.

We had the Swiss bliss burger and mac and cheese and the triple smash burger with skinny fries. Burgers were a good size, cooked well. Little underwhelming for what we were expecting as far as menu description and price point.
Fries were fries. The Mac and cheese, really liked it, best we have had in Da Nang so far.
Would go back to try some of the delicious looking desserts they have.

user_reviews.Name: Sergejs Ponomarjovs
user_reviews.Description:
Chops Da Nang

Chops is a small local burger place with a clear US-influenced interior. Simple setup, casual atmosphere, nothing overdesigned. You come here to eat, not to pose.

I ordered a beef burger and the quality of the meat was the standout. Proper beef taste, juicy, well cooked, no shortcuts. This is the main reason to come here.

The coleslaw deserves a mention. Fresh, crunchy, not overloaded with sauce, and actually works well as a side instead of being an afterthought.

Portions are solid, food feels honest and properly prepared. Service is straightforward and friendly, no drama, no upselling.

Overall, a great small local place if you want a good beef burger with decent ingredients and a relaxed American-style vibe. No hype, just good execution.

user_reviews.Name: Jay Sims
user_reviews.Description:
What a way to start your day!

We stopped here for brunch yesterday and were so impressed we decided to come back from breakfast today.

On both occasions, I had the smoothie Super Bowl, which was well put together and certainly hit the spot. My wife had the beetroot, feta, and walnut salad on the second visit.

The carrot and ginger juice was a new one on me, but enjoyable.

Very happy returning customers!

title: Benaras Central Da Nang
address: 207 Nguyễn Văn Thoại, An Hải, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Julia DH
user_reviews.Description:
I tried:
•	Paneer Tikka Masala
•	Chili Paneer Zing
•	White rice
•	Naan bread

The food was really delicious and suited my taste very well 😋
The paneer was soft and creamy, the curry was rich and flavorful, and the naan went perfectly with it. The Chili Paneer had a nice light spicy flavor with authentic Indian spices.

Special thanks to Ms. Vy for enthusiastically recommending the dishes to me ❤️ And Andy, the Indian staff member, was very funny and friendly, which made the dining experience even more enjoyable.

Overall, it was a warm and tasty experience. I would love to come back again 🥰

user_reviews.Name: drpal
user_reviews.Description:
If you’re visiting Da Nang and craving authentic Indian food, this restaurant is a great choice. The prices are slightly on the higher side, but the taste is excellent and very similar to what you would expect at a good Indian restaurant in India.

They have several branches across the city, making it a convenient option for travelers. Many tour groups also bring Indian tourists here for buffet breakfast, lunch, and dinner.

Highly recommended for authentic Indian cuisine, including delicious North Indian thalis, curries, naan, and a wide variety of vegetarian and non-vegetarian dishes. A dependable place to enjoy familiar flavors while exploring Vietnam.

user_reviews.Name: The Candid Critic
user_reviews.Description:
Benaras central in Da Nang is a spacious Indian restaurant with plenty of seating, so finding a table isn’t an issue even during busy hours. The place feels comfortable and well set up for both small groups and families.

We ordered the Masala Dosa, Chicken Thali Set, and Hara Bhara Kebab. The Masala Dosa was nicely done and enjoyable. The Chicken Thali Set was okay overall. It comes with rice, roti, and three to four curry options, giving a decent variety, though nothing particularly stood out. The Hara Bhara Kebab was good and probably one of the better items we ordered.

Overall, the food is good, but prices are slightly on the higher side compared to other options in Da Nang. It’s worth checking the menu and prices beforehand. A solid option if you’re craving Indian food and don’t mind paying a bit extra.

title: Burger House Da Nang(햄버거)
address: 253 D. Đình Nghệ, An Hải, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Christopher Aikens
user_reviews.Description: Absolutely fantastic burger spot in Da Nang! As an American, I’m pretty militant about my burgers — they’ve got to be done right — and Burger House absolutely nailed it. The cheeseburger was everything a proper burger should be: juicy, flavorful, and topped with just the right amount of cheese and dressing. Nothing overdone, just pure, delicious balance. I honestly had to stop myself from ordering a second one. The fries were perfectly crispy, and the strawberry smoothie was epic — fresh, creamy, and a great complement to the meal. The service was equally top-notch — friendly, quick, and attentive. Bad service can ruin even a great burger, but here everything was spot on. Highly recommended for anyone craving a top-quality burger done right!

user_reviews.Name: karla de la tejera
user_reviews.Description:
I'll start with what I liked. The fries are great. They are nice and crisp and seasoned with just the right amount of salt and thyme. They are served with a side of roasted garlic, which pairs very nicely. And of course, the staff is friendly.

I was not a fan of the meat's quality on the burger. This made me nervous because they cook the meat a little on the rare side (which I personally don't like in ground beef). In fact, the meat looked pale, and the cheese wasn't melted. And I'm pretty sure they didn't season the meat. The sauce on the burger helped, but overall it was a bit bland. I expected a good burger based on the reviews, but for me, that wasn't the case today.

user_reviews.Name: Todd Moon
user_reviews.Description: When I first arrived in Da Nang last year I was visiting the burger houses but this place was pretty far from my home and I never got a chance to visit it. Now I am happy to say I live 1 block away from the best burger house I have visited in Da Nang. As soon as I sat down this plate of amazing hand cut fries was delivered to my table. They are perfectly seasoned with Thyme a little salt and a chunck of baked garlic. 😳🙏The first thing I say always is the burger needs to be sloppy to bd good and this one checks that box off right off. I got the Cheese burger and added bacon. I took the baked garlic and it went right onto the burger. You can see too my fringer print in hhe very fresh bun. This burger stands toe to toe with any good hamburger in America. This place is hands down a must go to of you are craving a Hamburger while in Da Nang. This will be a regular stop when I beed my western food fix.

title: Fill My Buns

plus_code: 26XP+W9 Ngu Hanh Son, Da Nang, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Lwin Phyo
user_reviews.Description:
Tried Cheesy Beef Burger with Vietnamese Cà Phê Sữa at Fill My Bun today 🍔☕

The burger was really tasty — juicy beef, melted cheese, and fresh vegetables. Everything tasted well balanced and satisfying. The place is also very clean, which made the experience even better.

The staff girl was very friendly and good at communicating. She was smiling and welcoming, which made the service feel warm and comfortable.

The environment is peaceful with nice air conditioning, and the background music tone is just right — relaxing and enjoyable while eating.

Overall, a great place to enjoy a burger and coffee. I’d definitely come back again 👍

user_reviews.Name: Murad Sami
user_reviews.Description: A very cozy burger place tucked in a quiet street with delicious burgers! Looking for halal food options in Da Nang, this place really exceeded my expectations whilst hitting my burger craving just perfectly! The service from all staff was very welcoming and they are very good with explaining options should it be needed. I kindly had to ask if halal options were available and the staff took her time to explain all options available to make everything clear. Welcome drinks (tea) also provided following food order. I really enjoyed the double cheesy burger which I was able to order in a combo meal due to happy hour. The fries were also perfect with good seasoning and various sauce options were also available. Had to go with my favourite being ranch at it did not disappoint!

Dear Murad Sami,

user_reviews.Name: Calum
user_reviews.Description:
One of the best gluten free meals I’ve had in a long time. Staff were really knowledgeable and confirmed the fries are cooked in a separate fryer, which made me feel a lot more comfortable eating there.

The sticky rice bun was something I’d never tried before and it was honestly so good. The burger itself was cooked perfectly, full of flavour, and the FMB sauce was unreal.

Finished with the gluten free cheesecake and it topped the whole meal off perfectly. Best part was having absolutely no symptoms afterwards. Definitely recommend for anyone gluten free looking for a proper burger place.

user_reviews.Name: Mia
user_reviews.Description:
Absolutely blown away by Fill My Buns in Da Nang! 🍔✨

If you’re searching for a proper smash burger — juicy, perfectly seasoned, with those crispy edges that make every bite unforgettable — this is the place. The quality of the ingredients really shines through, and everything tasted incredibly fresh and made with care.

What makes it even better is that they offer halal options, which can sometimes be hard to find when travelling. It meant we could completely relax and enjoy our meal without any compromise — and trust me, it did not disappoint.

The owner was there during our visit and was genuinely super lovely — so welcoming and passionate about the food and customer experience. It really adds a personal touch that you don’t always find.

The restaurant itself is modern, spotless, and beautifully designed, creating a great atmosphere whether you’re dining in or grabbing a quick bite.

The Trio Advisor recommendation led us to a true gem. Friendly service, fantastic setting, and hands down one of the best burgers we’ve had on this trip. Whether you’re a burger lover or just craving comfort food done exceptionally well, Fill My Buns is a must-visit in Da Nang.

Already planning our next visit!

user_reviews.Name: Raghib Albalushi
user_reviews.Description:
I had a great experience at this café! The place is very cozy, clean, and comfortable. The staff treated us really well from the moment we stepped in. They even gave us a free welcome drink—cold jasmine tea, which was really refreshing!

They also asked if we eat halal food and showed us all the halal options available. The staff recommended some of their best-seller dishes, and everything we tried was delicious. I really appreciate how thoughtful and friendly they were.

I highly recommend this place, especially for anyone looking for good halal food. Great service, tasty food, and a warm atmosphere — definitely worth a visit!

user_reviews.Name: WHOS HOUS
user_reviews.Description:
I love my FILL MY BUNS SO much!

This is good food, fresh food, flavoursome and I’m very fussy when it comes to burgers in any part of the world.

But I have eaten here about 4 times now and I love their food especially this new burger they introduced on the menu, I devoured that shit fast! Haha 😂😭

The only thing is missing is a REAL homemade spicy chilli authentic sauce. The owner/manager was really cool and twice has given me his personal bottle of Piri Piri sauce he got from Portugal 😭🙏🏽 which just elevated the burger and fries more so I really wish they bring more because I’m sure I finished it all😭😭😭😭

The photos have two different type of burgers - both slap!

Thank you! I will be back again xxx

user_reviews.Name: Christopher Gorrie
user_reviews.Description:
I had a good experience at Fill My Buns, despite the name making me raise an eyebrow. The staff are really nice and friendly, and made me feel right at home. You place your order through a QR code and website that is very simple, and they have free WiFi.

I ordered the classic cheeseburger combo with fries, chili dipping sauce, added jalapeños, and got a lime Pepsi. The cheeseburger was really moist, and the tomato they use was high-quality (worth noting since so many burgers have bad tomatoes). Fluffy bun, good distribution of toppings, and the portion of fries was really big! The fries were also crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside, and had a yummy seasoning in them.

For 205,000 VND (~$7.81 USD) out the door, this was a solid deal for the quality and service. If you’re craving a burger in Da Nang, this spot is worth putting near the top of your list.

We’re happy you enjoyed the "solid deal" and look forward to welcoming you back to your new favorite burger spot in Da Nang!

user_reviews.Name: Alex Pyne
user_reviews.Description:
What an amazing experience I had here.
I landed into da nang after a long day of travelling & ordered this on grab. The waitress rang me and asked me was I coeliac & infomed me they’d take extra care (lovely to hear). She also apologised as I’d ordered the cheesecake that it had been super and she’d just taken it out the freezer & needed to thaw a little- no issues. Then I got this super cute note when the grab arrived. The burger was gorgeous, my first time having a rice bun & it was lovely & the cheesecake amazing too.

I ate here again, this time going to the restaurant. The waitress was really friendly & knowledgeable of gluten free- informed me that I could have only the fries as a side because of the gluten (no worries). The meal again was lovely. So nice to have somewhere you don’t need to worry about becoming ill from gluten.

I’d highly recommenced

title: Bon Bocon Restaurant
address: 164 Châu Thị Vĩnh Tế, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 50000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: mai kindle
user_reviews.Description:
My husband and I have been loyal customers for around 4 years even before the rebranding of Bon Bocon which replaced its old name as Torino.

The dishes are high quality but prices are quite affordable.

The chef is super friendly. She often greets us with a big smile and sometimes she takes our order since we pretty much order the same things every time.

We love the place! Can't recommend enough this amazing place to everyone!

user_reviews.Name: Cần Minh (Minkan)
user_reviews.Description: Absolutely loved this place! Bon Bocon is a total standout in the Da Nang food scene if you’re after legit Italian food without any of the stuffy vibes. The atmosphere is super chill and low-key, making it the perfect spot to just hang out and enjoy a high quality meal that hits the spot every time. It’s got that rare mix of top tier standards and a relaxed, welcoming energy that makes it an easy 10/10 for anyone looking for a great night out in the city.

user_reviews.Description:
We absolutely love this place and come here almost every single evening to get some pastries to go! The bakery items are incredibly delicious.
The owner is so wonderful and always welcomes us with a warm smile, making sure everything is perfect. You can really tell he genuinely cares about his business and his customers, unlike some places where it's just about the money. He is so involved in the daily work and really takes care of everyone. Highly recommend this spot for the amazing baked goods and the great atmosphere!

user_reviews.Name: KAĞAN İŞMEN
user_reviews.Description: This is a cafe, restaurant, and bakery where you could become a regular customer. It's especially fantastic for Europeans. Fresh bread comes out of the oven in the mornings. There's a wide variety. Breakfast has a lot of variety. Lunch and dinner also have plenty of options. The coffee and desserts are amazing. It's like an oasis in the desert. I definitely recommend it. They speak English and Italian. The owners are Italian and Vietnamese. Credit cards are accepted.

Delicious and Authentic!
​"I had a fantastic meal at Lazeez Indian Restaurant. The food was very good and truly authentic—you can tell they care about the flavors.
​A word of advice: the portion sizes are quite big, so be careful not to order too much! For drinks, I highly recommend the Mango Lassi and the Iced Lemon Tea; both were absolutely delicious and refreshing. Definitely worth a visit if you're looking for quality Indian food."

user_reviews.Name: dhaarna kukreja
user_reviews.Description: We were craving Indian food after five days of eating banh mi and pho. Thank god we found this place. We ordered chicken kadhai and biryani. ate our hearts out after being hungry for almost 12 hours. Good food. Courteous staff.

user_reviews.Name: SURAJ DAS
user_reviews.Description:
Lazeez Indian Restaurant, Da Nang.

Really one of the awesome Indian restaurant I have seen in vietnam. I was really amazed to find taste of the food to be exactly taste like that we have in India. Definitely the cooks are Indian, and all the spices they use is exactly like indian food.

I went there almost 4 times, every time I enjoyed the food. Not once I did have any complain.

Regarding the food apart from taste they really provide very good quantity according to the price they charged.

The service was really great, no complain. Staff was really nice and polite. They have Outdoor and Indoor Sitting. They have little game area as well. Pool table.

Must recommended for those who are in Da Nang. Visit atleast once.

title: Nhà Hàng Chay Tathata - Vegan Restaurant Da Nang
address: 56D Lê Hồng Phong, Hải Châu, Đà Nẵng 55000, Vietnam

owner.name: Nhà Hàng Chay Tathata - Vegan Restaurant Da Nang (Owner)

user_reviews.Name: Llobby Lloyd
user_reviews.Description: We absolutely loved everything about Nhà Hàng Chay Tathata. We ordered the fresh salad rolls, Pad Thai (which when we asked is the most popular dish on the menu) and the Bahn Xeo which was incredible. Can’t tell you how good it is. You won’t be disappointed. It is also BIG and a choice for sharing. There are lots of peanuts in every dish too, so be aware of that. Lots of hot chilli also, so also be careful if you don’t like it spicy. I also loved the chill vibe here and the great compassion manta on repeat. Top of the list if you’re in Da Nang!
user_reviews.text_original: We absolutely loved everything about Nhà Hàng Chay Tathata. We ordered the fresh salad rolls, Pad Thai (which when we asked is the most popular dish on the menu) and the Bahn Xeo which was incredible. Can’t tell you how good it is. You won’t be disappointed. It is also BIG and a choice for sharing. There are lots of peanuts in every dish too, so be aware of that. Lots of hot chilli also, so also be careful if you don’t like it spicy. I also loved the chill vibe here and the great compassion manta on repeat. Top of the list if you’re in Da Nang!

user_reviews.Name: Keith Walsh
user_reviews.Description:
The food here has always either been between just plain good to absolute excellent..
They seem to get great flavors going in all their dish's..
The Indian curry today was sublime, as was the winter pumpkin soup the previous day. And the day before that it was something else.
Squeezing their craft onto various plates and bowls, from a tiny kitchen is in itself some kind of magic trick.. let alone the quality that is implanted in each dish.
Great care is given to the presentation along with the layers of flavor and the textures.
Like when you get that wonderful perfume fragrance from a ripe red chili but not the burn from the seed because they removed it to give you the best part of that chili experience..
If one had to look for fault, I would say that the premises need a make over. Not that it's not without charm right now but it could be as good as the food and right now it's not.

user_reviews.Description:
I had the Tathata dish and it was sooooo good! A bit oily but in a good way, I really enjoyed my meal. Salted coffee was a bit sweet for my liking but still very enjoyable. Will come here again, and I recommend patronizing this shop to everyone craving a good meal, I'm sure non-vegetarians will enjoy as well.
The only further improvement I could suggest is to skip the plastic straws.

user_reviews.Name: Olga
user_reviews.Description: I just arrived and checked in, it was almost 11 pm, so of course I went to see if anything was open, and it turned out this was the only cafe! I'm not a fan of vegan cafes, but the menu here is very European, and the atmosphere is great. I figured everything was closed anyway, so I'd go here. This is the best place, so delicious. I didn't think I'd like this cafe so much. 🍽️, the staff and atmosphere are simply magical. 🤩, I will definitely come back. Now I'm a fan of vegan cafes. 🥑🤍🤍🙏🏻

user_reviews.Name: Namo
user_reviews.Description:
Every single bite is a delight.

This place has such a relaxing atmosphere, and the owners are incredibly kind and welcoming.

Even though I’m not vegan, I come here every day when I’m in the area. I enjoy discovering new restaurants, but this is without a doubt the best restaurant in Da Nang and one of the best I’ve ever been to.

Prices are very reasonable, the food is outstanding, and despite visiting many times, I was never asked to leave a review — which feels rare and very refreshing.

Don’t miss the My Quang, Tahata rice, and the spring rolls — the peanut sauce is to die for.

A true gem ❤️
user_reviews.text_original:
Every single bite is a delight.

This place has such a relaxing atmosphere, and the owners are incredibly kind and welcoming.

Even though I’m not vegan, I come here every day when I’m in the area. I enjoy discovering new restaurants, but this is without a doubt the best restaurant in Da Nang and one of the best I’ve ever been to.

Prices are very reasonable, the food is outstanding, and despite visiting many times, I was never asked to leave a review — which feels rare and very refreshing.

Don’t miss the My Quang, Tahata rice, and the spring rolls — the peanut sauce is to die for.

A true gem ❤️

user_reviews.Name: Felix
user_reviews.Description:
Just wow!
We came here, because we heard this is one of the best rated restaurants on Happy Cow all over the world. And now we know why!
In a country with delicious food in general, this one is outstanding! For us the best food we had in Vietnam so far in nearly two months.

user_reviews.Name: Eat the world
user_reviews.Description:
The vegan pho was genuinely excellent. For a moment I forgot the broth contained no meat flavors, which is a real compliment.

The bowl was generously filled with fresh, natural ingredients, and the side plate of sprouts was equally abundant and crisp.

This is a modest, worthy place serving flavorful Vietnamese food without animals on the plate.

user_reviews.Name: Chen Hsieh
user_reviews.Description:
The food here is absolutely delicious, and I genuinely enjoyed it.
However, I strongly recommend dismissing the current server.
I ordered a salad and spring rolls and waited for over 20 minutes. When I finally went up to ask why my meal hadn't arrived, the situation was ridiculous:
1. The waiter was simply slacking off. My order was apparently ready a long time ago, but she just didn't bother to bring it out.
2. After I complained, she only brought the salad.
3. I asked, "Where are my spring rolls?" She looked completely bewildered and actually told me that a vegan restaurant wouldn't sell spring rolls!
4. It wasn't until I pointed to the picture on the menu that she finally understood and rushed to the kitchen to get the second dish.
What exactly was she doing all that time? I have never seen a staff member be this lazy and incompetent.
Right now, another customer just walked in, and she is still fooling around at the counter.
What is the point of keeping a waiter like this? You need to fire her.

title: Öztürk Halal Turkish Restaurant
address: 254 Hồ Nghinh, An Hải, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Nhan Phan
user_reviews.Description:
The food is delicious and tastes very authentic. Even though it’s not on the menu, if you like a bit of heat with your pide or doner, definitely ask for the pickled chilis—they’re amazing!

Their Ayran is also rich and nice.

To top it off, the staff is well-trained and yes, the vibe is great!
user_reviews.text_original:

user_reviews.Name: Erik Benjamin
user_reviews.Description:
My visit to OzTurk was truly enjoyable from start to finish. We started off with traditional Turkish tea, which was rich, aromatic, and the perfect way to begin the meal. It immediately set the tone for an authentic and comforting dining experience.

We then ordered the Göniit Döner and the Portion Döner, and both dishes were absolutely delicious. The döner meat was perfectly seasoned, tender, and full of authentic Turkish flavor. Each bite was satisfying, fresh, and clearly prepared with care. The portions were generous and well-balanced, making the meal both filling and memorable.

What really elevated the experience was the excellent customer service. The staff was incredibly kind, attentive, and welcoming. They took the time to explain the menu, checked on us regularly, and made sure everything was to our liking without ever being intrusive. Their warmth and professionalism made us feel genuinely appreciated as guests.

Overall, OzTurk delivers outstanding food paired with top-notch service. If you’re looking for authentic Turkish cuisine served by friendly and attentive staff, this restaurant is definitely worth visiting. Highly recommended!

user_reviews.Name: Mams La Fætro
user_reviews.Description:
Turkish restaurant and near our hotel, of course we needed to try it.
Great tasting food, it tastes like restaurants in Türkiye. And the service is also great, but the price is at the high end compared to the portions and other vietnamese restaurants.

My wife did order their beef shawarma plate with rice and fries and one bread.
I did order one chicken dürüm and one Lahmacun to share with my wife. I dürüm was smaller than i thought so i ended ordering one more.
My adwise would be order the plate since 2 x dürüm is more expensive than one plate.

Next day we did visit it again to eat breakfast this time, where we both did order each a Lahmacun.

Both days they gave us free turkish tea. And second day the owner did give us free sample of beef shawarma so we could try it. So they got a great service.
You can pay with card without fees.

user_reviews.Description:
Absolutely the best Turkish food in Da Nang! 🇹🇷✨
Everything was incredibly fresh, flavorful, and perfectly seasoned. The meats were juicy, the bread was warm and soft, and the portions were very generous. You can really taste the authenticity it feels like real Turkish home cooking, not a tourist version.

The atmosphere was welcoming, and the staff were friendly and attentive, making the whole experience even better. If you’re craving genuine Turkish cuisine in Da Nang, this place is a must-visit. I’ll definitely be back again!

It’s a favourite of ours and we’ve tried almost everything on the menu - haven’t been disappointed yet. Absolutely must do.

user_reviews.Name: Suzanne L.
user_reviews.Description:
This is a neat little restaurant tucked away in a little lane. Found this place with good reviews and tried it myself.

It was empty as we went on 1st day of Jan at 12pm.

Service was wonderful. If you wish to order without any meat, they will prepare as you wish. Except for the fried spring roll, it was already prepared to be fried, thus there will be pork in it.

Highly recommended to try Pork Ba Minh, Beef Pho and Fried Spring Rolls. The mango salad we had, is not as good as from the hotel we stayed. Coffee also not as aromatic as another Vietnamese restaurant.

Anyway had a yummy but very heavy lunch as we ordered so many dishes to try.

Price wise on the high side as the place is located in a tourist area.

address: 212 Trần Phú, Hải Châu, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: M H
user_reviews.Description: Great Italian food in a cozy setting. They serve a basket of free appetizers which is really nice. The tomato bruschetta and minestrone soup were delicious, and I had the Rustic pizza with imported cold cuts and homemade sausage. This is one of the best pizzas I've had in my years of living in Da Nang, and they don't skimp on the toppings! I barely managed to finish it. My partner had the clam linguine pasta which was good as well. We will be back 👌

user_reviews.Name: Carlino
user_reviews.Description:
We truly recommend Limoncello Italian Restaurant in Da Nang.
We visited in the evening with our children and were extremely well welcomed, which made the experience even more special. The staff was kind, patient, and very friendly with the kids, making us feel comfortable and relaxed.

The food is excellent and truly authentic Italian. The pasta and pizzas are full of flavor, made with high-quality ingredients, and everything was beautifully prepared. The atmosphere is cozy and inviting, perfect for families as well as couples.

It’s not always easy to find a restaurant that combines great food with such a warm attitude toward children, especially at night, and Limoncello did this perfectly. Highly recommended, especially for families traveling with kids. 🍝

title: Nhà hàng Mr.Anh
address: 340 Ông Ích Khiêm, Thanh Khê, Đà Nẵng 590000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: 양태은
user_reviews.Description:
A Perfect Choice for Our 30th Wedding Anniversary Pool Party!
To celebrate our 30th wedding anniversary, we decided to host a private pool party at our villa. We ordered takeout from Mr. An, and we could not have been more satisfied with our choice.

The food was absolutely delicious and held up perfectly from lunch through dinner. The secret to the incredible flavor is the pairing: I highly recommend enjoying each dish strictly with its designated sauce. The difference it makes is on a whole new level! The balance between the dishes and their respective sauces was simply perfect.

The quality of the packaging was impressive, showing how much care they put into their service. They labeled every container with sticky notes to identify each dish and sauce, and even provided clear, friendly instructions in Korean on how to best enjoy the meal. It was so helpful and allowed us to fully savor every bite.
We were also deeply touched by their hospitality. From partially covering our Grab taxi fare to the restaurant, providing complimentary ice water, and even taking photos for us as a couple, their thoughtful gestures made our celebration truly special.

We specifically chose items that taste great even after cooling down for our pool party, and they were all fantastic. We enjoyed our takeout experience so much that we are already looking forward to visiting the restaurant in person for a dine-in experience next time.

user_reviews.Name: Wong Ha
user_reviews.Description: The crispy golden fried items are perfectly complemented by a tangy dipping sauce. The main dish, with its tender fish and fresh herbs, is light yet satisfying. The fried rice stands out with its distinct "wok hei" aroma, crowned by a perfectly runny sunny-side-up egg. The vibrant plating is visually appealing, highlighting the freshness of the ingredients. Each dish feels authentic, offering a delicious and immersive taste of vibrant Vietnamese street food culture. A truly satisfying culinary experience.

user_reviews.Name: Man Fung Tsui
user_reviews.Description: The fried items achieve perfect crispness without greasiness, while the main course's delicate fish harmonizes beautifully with aromatic herbs and crunchy vegetables. The fried rice, wok-tossed to smoky perfection, becomes a standout when enriched by the golden, runny egg yolk. Each component showcases Vietnam's culinary philosophy: balancing heat, acidity, and herbaceous notes. The vibrant plating—from stone-slab presentations to traditional porcelain—enhances the sensory journey. This collection doesn't just serve food; it tells a story of street food elegance and sophisticated simplicity. An unforgettable taste of Vietnam's soul.

user_reviews.Name: Russ Lacuata
user_reviews.Description:
Amazing dining experience here at Mr. Anh! So glad we stumbled upon this place by accident.

The restaurant is clean. Very quaint with a rustic environment. The staff are wonderful and provided amazing service. We ordered the Vietnamese Style Hotpot with Black Chicken and Ginseng. We also added the Turmeric Fish. The Hotpot was delicious! A perfect since it was thundering and raining hard outside. The broth was full flavor, it warm you inside and out. I love the veggies, fresh outbound the field. Thanks to the staff for preparing it for us and taught us how to properly eat it and enjoy the hot pot. The Turmeric Fish is melts in your mouth goodness.

Overall, very unforgettable dining experience here in Danang.
user_reviews.text_original:
Amazing dining experience here at Mr. Anh! So glad we stumbled upon this place by accident.

The restaurant is clean. Very quaint with a rustic environment. The staff are wonderful and provided amazing service. We ordered the Vietnamese Style Hotpot with Black Chicken and Ginseng. We also added the Turmeric Fish. The Hotpot was delicious! A perfect since it was thundering and raining hard outside. The broth was full flavor, it warm you inside and out. I love the veggies, fresh outbound the field. Thanks to the staff for preparing it for us and taught us how to properly eat it and enjoy the hot pot. The Turmeric Fish is melts in your mouth goodness.

Overall, very unforgettable dining experience here in Danang.

Our team always strives to provide excellent service, and it's heartwarming to know it made your visit even more special. We look forward to welcoming you back again soon!

user_reviews.Name: Prasant Bothra
user_reviews.Description:
Flavours of India in Da Nang is a must-visit for anyone craving authentic Indian food as well as Jain Food away from home. The food, hospitality, and overall experience make it feel like you are eating at a close friend’s house rather than a restaurant.

The owner has excellent knowledge of Indian cuisine and goes out of his way to make guests feel comfortable and welcomed. His warm behaviour and genuine interest in our experience made a huge difference, and the hospitality truly felt like **home**. As a lovely gesture, he served us complimentary coconut-flavoured peanuts, which were unique and very tasty.

We tried the aloo paratha, samosa, and dosa, and all of them were cooked perfectly. The aloo paratha was soft with a well-spiced filling, the samosas were crispy with delicious stuffing, and the dosa had a great texture and authentic flavour. Every dish tasted awesome and reminded us of proper Indian homemade food. Overall, highly recommended for anyone in Da Nang looking for real Indian flavours with homely treatment.

user_reviews.Name: The Candid Critic
user_reviews.Description:
We tried a Turkish restaurant and ordered the chicken platter with pita bread and falafel. Since we’ve lived in the Middle East, we’re fairly familiar with Middle Eastern flavors and have some idea about Turkish cuisine as well. Because of that, we had certain expectations, but the food felt very different from what we’re used to.

The overall taste was quite bland for us. The hummus, in particular, didn’t taste like hummus at all and lacked flavor. The falafel was very wet from the inside, which didn’t feel right and didn’t resemble the falafel we’re familiar with. The chicken also didn’t taste authentic to us and felt more like it was coated in something similar to a teriyaki style sauce, which was disappointing.

We also ordered pita bread, expecting the flat pita we’re used to, but instead it was more like a balloon style bread, which again didn’t match our expectations. We understand that Turkish food can be different from Middle Eastern food, and it’s possible this is closer to authentic Turkish style, but for us, based on our experience with Middle Eastern cuisine, the food didn’t hit the mark.

People who regularly eat Turkish food may appreciate it more, but for us, it felt too bland and not what we were hoping for.

The food was served quickly, and everything was so tasty — we had the mixed grill, lamb burek, fries, chai, and pineapple juice. When we left, it was raining heavily, and they were kind enough to walk us to our Grab with an umbrella. We’ll definitely come back before the end of our trip in Da Nang!
user_reviews.reply_text: Thank you so much. Enjoy your meal!

title: Kanpai Izakaya & Sushi
address: 92 Đỗ Bá, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam
plus_code: 362V+FP Ngu Hanh Son, Da Nang, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Viktoriya Nishchuk
user_reviews.Description: Amazing experience: excellent food and service. The food arrives quickly and is delicious, made with high quality products. We tried a variety of dishes and everything was exceptional! I totally recommend this place and definitely will visit again myself.
user_reviews.text_original: Amazing experience: excellent food and service. The food arrives quickly and is delicious, made with high quality products. We tried a variety of dishes and everything was exceptional! I totally recommend this place and definitely will visit again myself.

user_reviews.Name: tenishiah vilarino
user_reviews.Description: Best place for Japanese and overall dinner. Fresh food all round. Service was also amazing and restaurant was stunning
user_reviews.text_original: Best place for Japanese and overall dinner. Fresh food all round. Service was also amazing and restaurant was stunning

user_reviews.Name: Gali Karni
user_reviews.Description:
A cute place, we walked in and the staff has this tradition where everyone stops what they're doing and greets you, which I found really lovely. But in the same breath, the place had a smell of wet dog (?) that I caught right away and didn't vibe with at all.
We went upstairs and there was a waiting staff who took care of us and literally watched us all evening—which on one hand is really nice to get such warm and attentive service, but on the other hand, it's a bit uncomfortable to eat knowing we are the only two in the room, and that the waiter is literally standing a few inches away from us and watching us throughout the whole meal. We ordered sushi and ramen, which were perfectly fine and pretty average compared to other sushi spots we've eaten at in Vietnam, or specifically in Da Nang.
If you are looking for simplicity, a wide variety of dishes, and don't mind certain smells, this is definitely the sushi place for you.

title: Vị Nhà Kitchen ( Traditional food)
address: 04 Phạm Thiều, Sơn Trà, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

user_reviews.Name: Duc Thanh Nguyen
user_reviews.Description:
Found this hidden gem in Da Nang and honestly wish I came here earlier. The food at Vị Nhà Kitchen tasted exactly like a proper home-cooked Vietnamese meal — full of flavour, fresh ingredients, and everything came out hot and beautifully presented.

The braised dishes and soups were especially comforting, the kind of food that makes you slow down and enjoy every bite. The owner was incredibly warm, friendly, and passionate about the food — you can really feel the hospitality here. Staff were also super welcoming and made us feel comfortable the whole time.

Cozy atmosphere, fair prices, and authentic local taste without feeling too touristy. If you’re in Da Nang and want real traditional Vietnamese food, this place is 100% worth visiting. Will definitely come back next trip!

user_reviews.Name: Mean as feeds
user_reviews.Description: Went for dinner and it was really delicious and good value for money. Family owned local restaurant, nice and clean seating area. Fast and friendly service. Had the BÚN BÒ XÀO - Stir-fried Beef Vermicelli with a fried egg.

user_reviews.Name: Danila B
user_reviews.Description:
Interesting place in the area. It's very strange that I haven't seen it before. Nice family restaurant with great service and very tasty local food. Buncha is great, fried spring rolls are great. I will definitely come again to try Bun Thit Nuong )))
Update: Bun Thit Nuong is delicious 🔥👍
thank you!

P. S. Restaurant is being updating menu prices lately, just FYI. But still great quality food.

user_reviews.Description: Very yummy! We order the 5 piece chicken spring rolls and Bún Cha. It is perfect for breakfast! We are staying close by and have been here 3 times already in our week in Da Nang. Friendly staff!

user_reviews.reply_text_original: Vị Nhà Kitchen would like to express our sincere gratitude for your support and kindness. We always strive to provide delicious food and the best possible service to every customer.

user_reviews.Name: Jakob Reinehr
user_reviews.Description: I’ve been to Sala Vegan Food multiple times and tried almost every dish on the menu — everything is consistently excellent. The food is mostly Vietnamese, made with natural ingredients and prepared gently, which really brings out the flavors. One clear highlight is the Sea Grape Salad — fresh, unique, and beautifully presented. Other standouts include brown rice dishes with seasonal vegetables and the toasted bread with peanut butter and banana. Everything looks as good as it tastes. The two women who run the place are incredibly kind and welcoming. Prices are very fair. Highly recommended.
